  • Assist with testing, validation, and deployment of system enhancements and process improvements.
  • Create and maintain documentation, procedures, and work instructions related to master data and configuration processes.
  • Analyze data issues, identify root causes, and implement corrective actions to improve data quality.
  • Support user training and provide ongoing guidance regarding system functionality and best practices.
  • Participate in continuous improvement initiatives focused on process efficiency and data integrity.

Qualifications

Required

  • Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Manufacturing Systems, Information Systems, Business, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent combination of education and experience.
  • 3+ years of experience supporting ERP systems, master data management, product data management, or business systems administration.
  • Experience maintaining material master records, product structures, or BOMs.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Excellent organizational skills with strong attention to detail.
  • Effective written and verbal communication skills.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

Preferred

  • Experience with ERP platforms such as SAP, Oracle, JD Edwards, Microsoft Dynamics, Infor, Epicor, or similar systems.
  • Experience with product configurators, configure-to-order products, or rules-based product configuration tools.
  • Knowledge of manufacturing, supply chain, or operations processes.
  • Experience with data governance, data quality initiatives, and process documentation.
  • Advanced proficiency with Microsoft Excel and other Microsoft Office applications.
  • Experience supporting system implementations, upgrades, testing, or validation activities.
  • Familiarity with Lean, Continuous Improvement, or process optimization methodologies.

Desired Skills

  • ERP Configuration
  • Material Master Data Management
  • Bill of Materials (BOM) Management
  • Product Configuration
  • Data Governance
  • Business Systems Support
  • Manufacturing Operations
  • Process Improvement
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration
  • System Testing and Validation

What Success Looks Like

  • High level of master data accuracy and integrity.
  • Timely implementation of product and configuration changes.
  • Accurate BOM and product structure maintenance.
  • Strong partnership with manufacturing, supply chain, and engineering teams.
  • Continuous improvement of data management and configuration processes.

Title: SALESFORCE ANALYST
Description:
This position is not eligible for immigration sponsorship. All qualified candidates must be legally authorized to work in the United States without employer sponsorship.




Trident Seafoods is North America's largest vertically integrated seafood harvesting and processing company. Trident is a privately held, 100 percent USA-owned company with global operations in 6 countries and serves customers in almost 60 countries. Headquartered in Seattle, Washington (USA), Trident employs approximately 7,700 people worldwide each year and partners with over 2,700 independent fishermen and crewmembers in Alaska. Trident catches and processes virtually every commercial species of salmon, whitefish, and crab harvested in the North Pacific and Alaska. The global supply chain also encompasses cultured and wild species sourced from an international network of trusted suppliers.




Summary: The Salesforce Analyst supports the Sales Operations organization by delivering scalable reporting, analytics, and insights from Salesforce. This role partners closely with Sales leadership and Sales Support to translate CRM data into actionable insights that drive performance, adoption, and data quality. The Salesforce Analyst also provides secondary Salesforce configuration support and administration ensuring continuity and redundancy across the Sales Operations team.




Essential Functions:

Design, build, and maintain Salesforce reports and dashboards to support sales performance, pipeline management, forecasting, and leadership decision-making.
Analyze Salesforce data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities related to sales execution, pipeline health, and customer engagement.
Partner with Sales leadership and Sales Support to define metrics, KPIs, and standard performance views used across the organization.
Monitor Salesforce data quality and proactively identify issues related to usage, data completeness, and adherence to business processes.
Support sales enablement and adoption efforts by providing insights into Salesforce usage and effectiveness.
Provide secondary Salesforce administrative support, including basic configuration such as fields, record types, page layouts, and simple automation.
Collaborate closely with the Salesforce Administrator to understand system architecture, data models, and governance standards.
Support cross-functional analytics needs by extracting and analyzing Salesforce data to complement broader commercial insights.
Document reporting logic, metric definitions, and dashboards to ensure consistency and scalability.
Regularly works onsite to collaborate cross functionally, strengthen partnerships, and drive real time problem solving.




The essential functions listed above are not exhaustive. The organization may assign other duties as needed to meet business requirements. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform these essential functions.




Compensation & Benefits:

This is a salaried exempt position with an annualized salary range of $100,000 to $120,000.




Trident Seafoods offers a comprehensive and quality benefits package. Full time employees may be eligible for discretionary/performance-based incentives, medical, dental and vision insurance plans, optional HRA/HSA, telemedicine, employee assistance and wellness programs, disability programs, basic life and AD&D, and a 401(k) plan with a company match, paid time off (PTO), 10 paid holidays each year, and paid parental leave. For full-time employees, the initial PTO benefit starts at 20 days per year, adjusted commensurate with relevant experience, front loaded on the first of the year, initially prorated quarterly based on hire date. Commuter programs are also available.




More information can be found at https://www.tridentseafoods.com/benefits.




Required Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in business, analytics, information systems, computer science, or a related field; or an equivalent combination of education and relevant work experience.
A minimum of 2 years of experience working with Salesforce in an analytics, operations, or reporting capacity.
Demonstrated experience building Salesforce reports and dashboards to support sales performance, pipeline management, and leadership decision-making.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate CRM data into actionable insights for Sales and Sales Support stakeholders.




Preferred Education & Experiences:

Experience supporting Sales, Sales Operations, or Revenue Operations teams.
Experience with Salesforce administration or configuration, such as fields, page layouts, record types, or basic automation.
Exposure to SQL, Power BI, Tableau, or similar analytics and visualization tools.
Experience working with large, complex datasets and standardizing metrics across teams.
Experience in a CPG, foodservice, retail, or similarly complex commercial environment.




Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities (KSAs):

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to identify trends, risks, and opportunities in sales data.
Solid understanding of CRM data structures and how Salesforce supports sales processes and workflows.
Ability to communicate insights clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
High attention to detail with a strong focus on data accuracy, consistency, and usability.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver high-quality work in a fast-paced environment.
Strong collaboration skills and the ability to work effectively with Sales leadership, Sales Support, and cross-functional partners.
Self-motivated, eager to learn, and able to operate with increasing autonomy over time.




Work Environment:

This is an on-site position based at the Trident Support Center office in Seattle’s Ballard neighborhood, with the option to work remotely on Fridays.




In-office time takes place in a typical office environment: temperature controlled with adequate lighting and moderate noise. While remote, employees must provide appropriate home office space.




Physical demands:

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to stand, walk and sit; talk or hear, both in person and by telephone; use hands to finger, handle or feel objects or controls; reach with hands and arms; stoop, kneel, or crouch.




Work authorizations:

This position is not eligible for immigration sponsorship.

Title: Analyst, Business Intelligence
Description:
At T-Mobile, we invest in YOU! Our Total Rewards Package ensures that employees get the same big love we give our customers. All team members receive a competitive base salary and compensation package - this is Total Rewards. Employees enjoy multiple wealth-building opportunities through our annual stock grant, employee stock purchase plan, 401(k), and access to free, year-round money coaches. That’s how we’re UNSTOPPABLE for our employees!


Job Overview

This position is responsible for analyzing and reporting on the performance of advertising campaigns for T-Mobile Advertising Solutions and its clients.

We're adding a skilled data analyst who excels in uncovering insights from various datasets, translating information into actionable recommendations, and optimizing advertising performance through data-driven strategies.

As part of the Blis Platform organization, you will work in tandem with Customer Success and Ad Operations to provide insights that enhance campaign effectiveness and inform strategic decisions.

Job Responsibilities:


Develop and maintain dashboards and reports to track KPIs, including CTR, CPA, ROAS, conversion rates, and more.
Partner with Customer Success and Sales teams to support client presentations and business reviews.
Communicate insights and recommendations to both technical and non-technical stakeholders in clear and actionable ways to enhance campaign outcomes.
Respond and produce additional ad hoc reporting analysis requests and assignments as required.
Also responsible for other duties/projects as assigned by business management as needed



Education and Work Experience:


Bachelor's Degree plus 2 years of related work experience OR combination of education and experience deemed equivalent (Required)
Acceptable areas of study include Business, Statistics, Economics, Marketing or related field. (Preferred)
Prefer 2+ years experience related to business intelligence. (Preferred)
Analyst experience in a publisher, advertising agency, or ad-tech (Preferred)
Experience in conducting and communicating data-driven analyses; proven experience with quantitative and qualitative analysis and report writing. (Required)
Experience with data analysis tools such as SQL, Google Analytics, or BI tools (Tableau, Looker, Power BI) (Required)



Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:


Excellent writing, editing and proofreading skills. (Required)
Exceptional attention to detail and critical thinking skills. (Required)
Excellent communication skills. Comfortable interacting with and presenting to management, and peers in various departments. (Required)
Strong quantitative analysis skills and high proficiency with all Microsoft Office applications. (Required)
Expertise in Excel, PowerPoint and Word required. (Required)



Licenses/Certifications:

One or more: DMP, SSP, DSPs relevant certification; Google Ad Manager certification, The Trade Desk programmatic ads certification, etc. (Preferred)


At least 18 years of age
Legally authorized to work in the United States



Travel:

Travel Required (Yes/No):

DOT Regulated:

DOT Regulated Position (Yes/No): No

Safety Sensitive Position (Yes/No): No

Base Pay Range: $66,900 - $120,600

Corporate Bonus Target: 10%

The pay range above is the general base pay range for a successful candidate in the role. The successful candidate’s actual pay will be based on various factors, such as work location, qualifications, and experience, so the actual starting pay will vary within this range.

At T-Mobile, employees in regular, non-temporary roles are eligible for an annual bonus or periodic sales incentive or bonus, based on their role. Most Corporate employees are eligible for a year-end bonus based on company and/or individual performance and which is set at a percentage of the employee’s eligible earnings in the prior year. Certain positions in Customer Care are eligible for monthly bonuses based on individual and/or team performance. At T-Mobile, our benefits exemplify the spirit of One Team, Together! A big part of how we care for one another is working to ensure our benefits evolve to meet the needs of our team members. We cover all of the bases, offering medical, dental and vision insurance, a flexible spending account, 401(k), employee stock grants, employee stock purchase plan, paid time off and up to 12 paid holidays - which total about 4 weeks for new full-time employees and about 2.5 weeks for new part-time employees annually - paid parental and family leave, family building benefits, back-up care, enhanced family support, childcare subsidy, tuition assistance, college coaching, short- and long-term disability, voluntary AD&D coverage, voluntary accident coverage, voluntary life insurance, voluntary disability insurance, and voluntary long-term care insurance. We don't stop there - eligible employees can also receive mobile service & home internet discounts, pet insurance, and access to commuter and transit programs! Title: Business Analyst - Supplier Connectivity - Costco Travel
Description:
Costco Travel IT is responsible for the technical future of Costco Wholesale , the third largest retailer in the world with wholesale operations in fourteen countries. Despite our size and explosive international expansion, we continue to provide a family, employee centric atmosphere in which our employees thrive and succeed.

This is an environment unlike anything in the high-tech world and the secret of Costco’s success is its culture. The value Costco puts on its employees is well documented in articles from a variety of publishers including Bloomberg and Forbes. Our employees and our members come FIRST. Costco is well known for its generosity and community service and has won many awards for its philanthropy. The company joins with its employees to take an active role in volunteering by sponsoring many opportunities to help others.

Come join the Costco Travel IT family. Costco IT is a dynamic, fast-paced environment, working through exciting transformation efforts. We are building the next generation retail environment where you will be surrounded by dedicated and highly professional employees.

Business Analysts are responsible for eliciting and documenting requirements that are clear, concise, and testable. Business Analysts define a set of requirements that translate into solutions and technology systems, and communicate these to the client and other stakeholders. Business Analysts work closely with end users, business unit representatives, Information System resources, and others, in all stages of the project lifecycle from beginning to end. Business Analysts work with management to identify potential improvement areas.

The Supplier Connectivity Analyst documents, tracks, analyzes, and reports out key performance indicators to help determine the health, integrity, and data flow of partner integrations and web services. The Supplier Connectivity Analyst works with management to identify potential improvement areas, defines requirements, and communicates solutions for integrated software systems. The Supplier Connectivity Analyst provides external support to our partners and internal support to business, technology, and other stakeholders across all stages of the software development lifecycle (SDLC), ensuring continuous system monitoring and optimization.

If you want to be a part of one of the worldwide BEST companies “to work for”, simply apply and let your career be reimagined.

ROLE


Possesses product knowledge and process improvement skills, with a demonstrated ability to effectively propose solutions.
Defines and communicates requirements, risks, and issues that may require action.
Identifies, builds, and manages stakeholder relationships (i.e., business partners, users, vendors, customers, project team members, and management leaders).
Assists the development team with the translation of business requirements into functional or non-functional specifications.
Builds and maintains reports, dashboards, and metrics to monitor and analyze the performance of our vendors and systems.
Prepares materials in a clear, explicit, and presentable format to report out to stakeholders.
Develops individual and team business analysis plans, to manage activities, tasks, deliverables, and schedules.
Builds solutions/workflows to identify errors or exceptions with the vendor connection and coordinates with appropriate teams to correct the issue and prevent future occurrences.
Identifies opportunities for streamlining and increasing effectiveness through continuous process improvement.
Works with business, vendors, and technology groups to coordinate testing and implementations.
Participates in planning sessions to outline implementation requirements prior to deployment.
Provides first-level support for problems and issues as it relates to vendor connections.
Conducts ad-hoc reporting and analysis that drive high-impact business results.
Identifies and recommends new vendor partners based on the needs of the business.
Works independently and manages multiple projects and workflows simultaneously with minimal supervision.
Assists with other assigned special projects.


REQUIRED


Analytical, detail-oriented, and demonstrates the ability to research and problem-solve effectively.
Ability to compare and review data for inconsistencies; ability to communicate and understand root causes and take lead on resolution; ability to engage other teams as necessary.
Possesses the ability to communicate effectively, both written and verbal, with teammates, suppliers, cross-functional teams, and with all organizational levels of Costco.
Previous experience successfully managing multiple projects and tasks; excellent organizational skills and the ability to prioritize across competing priorities and deadlines.
Willingness to take direction and coaching from management and peers; values and demonstrates excellent and positive team interaction.
Leads by example and promotes a constructive approach to challenging issues
Flexible and adaptable to changes.
Proven experience in critical thinking in solving complex business problems, consistent with a deep understanding of the strategic direction of the business.


Recommended


3+ years’ Costco Travel experience.
Bachelor's Degree in Business Analysis, Business, Information Technology, or equivalent preferred.
Demonstrated success with initiating and driving projects to completion with minimal guidance.
Ability to comprehend and interpret XML, JSON, or related data exchange formats.
Experience with basic SQL queries and Splunk queries.
Experience with basic Tableau Report generation.
Experience with both Google and Microsoft O365 products.
Proficient in Microsoft Workspace applications, including Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Teams.


Required Documents


Cover Letter
Resume


California applicants, please click to review the Costco Applicant Privacy Notice.

Pay Ranges

Level 1 - $33.65 - $45.67

Level 2 - $90,000 - $120,000

Level 3 - $110,000 - $145,000

We offer a comprehensive package of benefits including paid time off, health benefits - medical/dental/vision/hearing aid/pharmacy/behavioral health/employee assistance, health care reimbursement account, dependent care assistance plan, short-term disability and long-term disability insurance, AD&D insurance, life insurance, 401(k), stock purchase plan to eligible employees.

Costco is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace. Costco is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or any other legally protected status. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or the recruiting process, please send a request to IT-Recruiting@costco.com

If hired, you will be required to provide proof of authorization to work in the United States. Applicants and employees for this position will not be sponsored for work authorization, including, but not limited to H1-B visas.

  • Identifies, builds, and manages stakeholder relationships (i.e., business partners, users, vendors, customers, project team members, and management leaders).
  • Assists the development team with the translation of business requirements into functional or non-functional specifications.
  • Builds and maintains reports, dashboards, and metrics to monitor and analyze the performance of our vendors and systems.
  • Prepares materials in a clear, explicit, and presentable format to report out to stakeholders.
  • Develops individual and team business analysis plans, to manage activities, tasks, deliverables, and schedules.
  • Builds solutions/workflows to identify errors or exceptions with the vendor connection and coordinates with appropriate teams to correct the issue and prevent future occurrences.
  • Identifies opportunities for streamlining and increasing effectiveness through continuous process improvement.
  • Works with business, vendors, and technology groups to coordinate testing and implementations.
  • Participates in planning sessions to outline implementation requirements prior to deployment.
  • Provides first-level support for problems and issues as it relates to vendor connections.
  • Conducts ad-hoc reporting and analysis that drive high-impact business results.
  • Identifies and recommends new vendor partners based on the needs of the business.
  • Works independently and manages multiple projects and workflows simultaneously with minimal supervision.
  • Assists with other assigned special projects.

REQUIRED

  • Analytical, detail-oriented, and demonstrates the ability to research and problem-solve effectively.
  • Ability to compare and review data for inconsistencies; ability to communicate and understand root causes and take lead on resolution; ability to engage other teams as necessary.
  • Possesses the ability to communicate effectively, both written and verbal, with teammates, suppliers, cross-functional teams, and with all organizational levels of Costco.
  • Previous experience successfully managing multiple projects and tasks; excellent organizational skills and the ability to prioritize across competing priorities and deadlines.
  • Willingness to take direction and coaching from management and peers; values and demonstrates excellent and positive team interaction.
  • Leads by example and promotes a constructive approach to challenging issues
  • Flexible and adaptable to changes.
  • Proven experience in critical thinking in solving complex business problems, consistent with a deep understanding of the strategic direction of the business.

Recommended

  • 3+ years’ Costco Travel experience.
  • Bachelor's Degree in Business Analysis, Business, Information Technology, or equivalent preferred.
  • Demonstrated success with initiating and driving projects to completion with minimal guidance.
  • Ability to comprehend and interpret XML, JSON, or related data exchange formats.
  • Experience with basic SQL queries and Splunk queries.
  • Experience with basic Tableau Report generation.
  • Experience with both Google and Microsoft O365 products.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Workspace applications, including Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Teams.

Required Documents

  • Cover Letter
  • Resume

California applicants, please click to review the Costco Applicant Privacy Notice.

Title: Principal Data Analyst - D&D
Description:
We take play seriously. We’re looking for curious adventurers ready to find their party, fueled by imagination and drive to build what’s never been built before. At Hasbro and Wizards of the Coast, you’ll collaborate with passionate teams to reimagine our iconic brands and create experiences that spark joy, connection, and community through the magic of play. This is your chance to shape legendary play that lasts a lifetime.




At Wizards of the Coast, we connect people around the world through play and imagination. From our genre-defining games like Magic: The Gathering® and Dungeons & Dragons® to our growing multiverse, we continue to innovate and build new ways to foster friendship and connection. That's where you come in!




The Principal Data Analyst, Dungeons & Dragons will modernize our forecasting capability and help lead demand planning for the brand-transitioning from trend- and judgment-based methods to a bottom-up approach that improves in-year accuracy and decision-making. This role partners closely with Product, Commercial, Finance, and IT to build scalable forecasting models, operationalize reliable data signals, and implement a repeatable forecasting process with strong governance, transparency, and adoption.




This is a hybrid role requiring three days a week in our Renton, WA office.




What You'll Do

Build, validate, and maintain demand forecasting models that incorporate leading indicators and key drivers across channels (product launches, promotions, pricing, platform/channel mix, marketing inputs, seasonality, and supply/transit constraints).
Partner with IT/data engineering to define and operationalize data pipelines (ingestion, transformation, QA, monitoring, documentation) and set data quality standards to ensure reliable forecast inputs.
Partner with Product and Finance teams highlighting forecast drivers, changes vs. long range plan, key risks/opportunities, and recommended actions to expand and improve the D&D product portfolio.
Own the D&D commercial "single view" across physical, D&D Beyond (subs + D2C where applicable), video games, and licensing/royalties-standardizing important metric definitions, building executive scorecards, and ensuring accurate inputs from partner organizations.




What You'll Bring

4+ years of experience leading detailed forecasting work or statistical analysis with demonstrated impact on planning and decision-making.
Proficiency in SQL and statistical programming in Python or R, plus strong Excel skills for business-facing analysis and reconciliation.
Experience working with modern data stacks and analytics workflows (e.g., cloud data warehouses like Snowflake; scheduled pipelines; QA/monitoring practices).
Strong partner management and communication skills; ability to drive alignment across Product Designers, Finance, IT, and commercial partners and communicate clearly to executive audiences.




Nice To Have

Experience in revenue operations, demand forecasting, commercial analytics, or data science in a complex commercial environment.
Background in consumer products, retail/eCommerce, subscriptions, entertainment, games, or adjacent industries.
Interest in (or curiosity about) Dungeons & Dragons and a willingness to learn the product ecosystem and customer motivations.




We are an Equal Opportunity / Affirmative Action Employer

Wizards of the Coast is committed to equality of opportunity in all aspects of employment. We are committed to making all employment decisions without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, marital status, or any other legally protected status.




We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. If you have a disability and require assistance in this application process and need to request an accommodation, please contact your recruiter or coordinator.




The pay transparency range for this role is listed below. The hiring range will vary based on factors such as experience, skills, location and market conditions. Additionally, employees may be eligible for annual and long-term incentives as part of their overall compensation package.

Pay Range: $160,300 USD - $240,500 USD




Employees may be eligible for annual and long-term incentives as part of their overall compensation package, depending on role, location, and eligibility. Benefits and programs may include:

Health & Wellness
Time Off to Recharge
Financial Well-being
Life & Family Support
Volunteer and Community Initiatives
Learning & Development
Exclusive Perks




Please review our Applicant Privacy Notice to learn how we collect, use, and protect your personal information in connection with the application process.

Title: AI Sales Intelligence Analyst
Description:
At F5, we strive to bring a better digital world to life. Our teams empower organizations across the globe to create, secure, and run applications that enhance how we experience our evolving digital world. We are passionate about cybersecurity, from protecting consumers from fraud to enabling companies to focus on innovation.

Everything we do centers around people. That means we obsess over how to make the lives of our customers, and their customers, better. And it means we prioritize a diverse F5 community where each individual can thrive.


This position is located in Seattle, WA**.
We are not considering remote employees at this time. Thank you.***



Position Summary

We're looking for a builder-minded analytics professional to join F5's Sales Business Intelligence team and help develop AI-powered analytics experiences for our global sales organization. This role sits at the intersection of data engineering, applied AI, and sales operations — and, equally important, at the intersection of the many partner teams that make sales intelligence possible. You will spend as much time listening to sales leaders, account teams, and cross-functional partners as you do writing code: translating ambiguous business questions into well-scoped agents, semantic models, and data products, and then clearly explaining what these systems can and cannot do.

You will help design, build, and maintain conversational analytics agents that allow sales leadership and executives to interrogate revenue, pipeline, and territory data in natural language. You will contribute to new agent builds and integrations — connecting our SharePoint, Salesforce, Snowflake, and Databricks ecosystems into a unified sales intelligence layer using modern enterprise AI platforms, agent frameworks, and related tooling. This is a strong opportunity for an early-career analyst or engineer who combines hands-on AI/LLM experience with strong communication and collaboration instincts.

Primary Responsibilities

Partner with Sales and Cross-Functional Teams


Serve as a trusted, approachable partner to Sales Operations, Finance, IT, and Sales Leadership — listening carefully to business questions and translating them into well-scoped agents, tools, and semantic model extensions
Run working sessions, demos, and user feedback loops with sales users to shape AI products that actually get adopted
Build executive-ready written materials, talking points, and demo narratives that explain complex AI systems in clear, non-technical language



Build and Maintain AI-Powered Sales Intelligence


Build and maintain conversational AI agents that answer sales, pipeline, bookings, discounting, and territory questions for the global sales organization
Contribute to new agent builds and support integrations between enterprise AI platforms and our SharePoint, Salesforce, Snowflake, and Databricks systems
Develop agent components using modern agent frameworks such as the Agent Development Kit (ADK), including specialized analyst sub-agents, tools, and connectors to BigQuery, Snowflake, and Salesforce data
Author and iterate on YAML semantic models, retrieval pipelines, and vector search indexes that ground agent responses in trusted, governed sales data
Build and maintain data pipelines in Snowflake, Databricks, and BigQuery that feed agent tools, embeddings, and evaluation datasets



Ensure Quality, Governance, and Adoption


Contribute to evaluation of agent quality, routing accuracy, and response faithfulness, including building and maintaining regression test cases
Collaborate with IT and Security teams to support security reviews, threat modeling inputs, and production deployment of AI applications
Help enable sales users on new AI tools through documentation, onboarding sessions, office hours, and day-to-day user support
Bring awareness to data quality, integrity, and accessibility issues and support efforts to resolve them



Other


Uphold F5's Business Code of Ethics and promptly report violations of the Code or other company policies
Perform additional duties as assigned



Knowledge, Skills And Abilities

Communication Excellence


Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to convey complex technical AI concepts in clear, concise language for non-technical audiences
Comfort presenting work, demos, and findings to peers, senior stakeholders, and on occasion executive leadership
Ability to produce clear documentation, enablement materials, and user-facing written communications



Collaboration and Partnership


Exceptional collaboration instincts, with the ability to build trusted working relationships across sales, operations, finance, IT, and engineering functions
Strong listener — able to sit with ambiguous business problems, ask good questions, and translate what they hear into concrete technical scope
Comfortable working under time constraints and iterating on ambiguous problems with guidance from more senior team members



Applied AI and Agent Building


Hands-on experience building with LLMs, including prompt engineering, tool use / function calling, and retrieval-augmented generation — in a professional, academic, or substantive personal project setting
Exposure to at least one agent framework such as Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK), LangGraph, LangChain, Vertex AI Agent Builder, or Snowflake Cortex Agents
Working understanding of enterprise AI and data governance concepts — including access controls, PII handling, and responsible AI practices — or a clear eagerness to learn



Data and Technical Proficiency


Solid SQL skills and working experience with a cloud data warehouse such as Snowflake, BigQuery, or Databricks
Proficiency in Python for data work and API integrations; familiarity with full-stack development (FastAPI, Next.js, or similar) is a plus
Familiarity with at least one major cloud platform: Google Cloud (Vertex AI, BigQuery) or Microsoft Azure (Azure AD, Key Vault, Logic Apps)
Experience working with CRM or enterprise business data, such as Salesforce opportunity, forecasting, or territory data, is a plus
Experience with a data visualization tool such as Tableau, Power BI, or Looker, and an eye for clear, accurate presentation of data
Experienced in Microsoft Office and collaborative tools (SharePoint, Teams)



Qualifications


BA/BS degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field required; coursework or academic projects in AI, machine learning, or applied LLM systems is a strong plus
2–3 years of professional experience in data analytics, data engineering, analytics engineering, or applied AI/ML; equivalent combinations of relevant coursework, research, internships, or substantial personal projects on AI systems will also be considered
Any hands-on experience building LLM or agent-based applications — professional, academic, or personal — is strongly preferred
Experience supporting a sales, revenue operations, or comparable GTM organization is a plus, not a requirement



The Job Description is intended to be a general representation of the responsibilities and requirements of the job. However, the description may not be all-inclusive, and responsibilities and requirements are subject to change.

The annual base pay for this position is: $77,600.00 - $116,400.00

F5 maintains broad salary ranges for its roles in order to account for variations in knowledge, skills, experience, geographic locations, and market conditions, as well as to reflect F5’s differing products, industries, and lines of business. The pay range referenced is as of the time of the job posting and is subject to change.

You may also be offered incentive compensation, bonus, restricted stock units, and benefits. More details about F5’s benefits can be found at the following link: https://www.f5.com/company/careers/benefits. F5 reserves the right to change or terminate any benefit plan without notice.

Please note that F5 only contacts candidates through F5 email address (ending with @f5.com) or auto email notification from Workday (ending with f5.com or @myworkday.com).

Equal Employment Opportunity

It is the policy of F5 to provide equal employment opportunities to all employees and employment applicants without regard to unlawful considerations of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, sensory, physical, or mental disability, marital status, veteran or military status, genetic information, or any other classification protected by applicable local, state, or federal laws. This policy applies to all aspects of employment, including, but not limited to, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, discipline, and termination. F5 offers a variety of reasonable accommodations for candidates. Requesting an accommodation is completely voluntary. F5 will assess the need for accommodations in the application process separately from those that may be needed to perform the job. Request by contacting accommodations@f5.com.

Title: Analyst - Supply Chain II
Description:
Application Close Date

Applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis until the requisition is closed.

At Blue Origin, we envision millions of people living and working in space for the benefit of Earth. We’re working to develop reusable, safe, and low-cost space vehicles and systems within a culture of safety, collaboration, and inclusion. Join our team of problem solvers as we add new chapters to the history of spaceflight! 

This role is part of Blue Origin Operations, which is comprised of Integrated Supply Chain, Test Operations, Safety, Quality, and Mission Assurance. This includes Manufacturing and Supply Chain support across all Blue Origin facilities.

Description: 

As part of a team of experts, you will support the design, development and fabrication of space launch vehicles and components to support human spaceflight programs. By providing analytical support to inform leadership decision-making you will share in the team’s impact on all aspects of production and manufacturing, so relevant experience in those areas is highly desired. We are seeking a individual with excellent oral, written, interpersonal, and analytics skills who can thrive in a fast-paced environment. This position will directly impact the history of space exploration and will require your commitment and detailed attention towards safe and repeatable spaceflight.

                                                                                      

Responsibilities


Support business unit financial, operational, and strategic objectives
Develop and deliver content for strategic reviews with internal leadership
Source, aggregate and organize manufacturing content sourced through enterprise data warehouses or sourced through stakeholder input.  Design and push that content to display monitors throughout the campus.
Partner with support organizations to optimize the production ramp while meeting quality, cost, schedule requirements
Build and maintain tools, analytics, and reporting to highlight performance metrics and other key information
Assist with creation of models for financial reviews, cost benefit analysis, and metrics
Perform risk & opportunity analysis and provide potential solutions. Work with the team to develop recovery plans
Identify opportunities for capacity enhancement, factory/work-center efficiencies, production planning and supply chain optimization
Data mining across multiple data sets
Lead key business analytics initiatives, as required


Qualifications


2+ years of business analysis or consulting experience
2+ years of experience in a manufacturing and/or supply chain driven environment.
Experience working with Business Intelligence tools (e.g. Tableau)
Exceptional with Excel and manual data analysis
Proficiency with MS Office suite including MS Access
Excellent written communication and presentation skills
Excellent Communication Skills
Understanding of data structures and architecture
Aptitude for teamwork with demonstrated potential for leadership
Proven ability to work simultaneously on multiple projects
Comfortable in a fast-paced, dynamic environment
Must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.


Desired


Experience with Visual Basic, Javascript, or Python programming
Understanding of manufacturing processes, maintenance processes, and industrial equipment
Experience using and developing within Atlassian Confluence and JIRA instances.
MBA or advanced degree
Greenfield experience


Compensation Range For

WA applicants is $99,201.00 - $138,880.35

Other Site Ranges May Differ

Culture Statement

Don’t meet all desired requirements? Studies have shown that some people are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single desired qualification. At Blue Origin, we are dedicated to building an authentic workplace, so if you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every desired qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.

Export Control Regulations

Applicants for employment at Blue Origin must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e. current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

Background Check


Required for all positions: Blue’s Standard Background Check
Required for Certain Job Profiles: Defense Biometric Identification System (DBIDS) background check if at any time the role requires one to be on a military installation
Required for Certain Job Profiles: Drivers who operate Commercial Motor Vehicles with a Gross Vehicle Weight (GVW), Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVWR) or combination of power unit and trailer that meets or exceeds 10,001 lbs. and/or transports placardable amounts of hazardous materials by ground in any vehicle on a public road while in commerce, may be subject to additional Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations including: Driver Qualification Files, Medical Certification (obtained before onboarding), Road Test, Hours of Service, Drug and Alcohol Testing (CDL drivers only), vehicle inspection requirements, CDL requirements (if applicable) and hazardous materials transportation/shipping training.
Required for certain Job Profiles: Ability to obtain and maintain Merchant Mariner Credential, which includes pre-employment and random drug testing as well as DOT physical


Benefits


Benefits include: Medical, dental, vision, basic and supplemental life insurance, paid parental leave, short and long-term disability, 401(k) with a company match of up to 5%, and an Education Support Program.
Stock Options for all regular employees (working at least 20 hours/week)

At Blue Origin, we envision millions of people living and working in space for the benefit of Earth. We’re working to develop reusable, safe, and low-cost space vehicles and systems within a culture of safety, collaboration, and inclusion. Join our team of problem solvers as we add new chapters to the history of spaceflight! 

This role is part of Blue Origin Operations, which is comprised of Integrated Supply Chain, Test Operations, Safety, Quality, and Mission Assurance. This includes Manufacturing and Supply Chain support across all Blue Origin facilities.

Description: 

As part of a team of experts, you will support the design, development and fabrication of space launch vehicles and components to support human spaceflight programs. By providing analytical support to inform leadership decision-making you will share in the team’s impact on all aspects of production and manufacturing, so relevant experience in those areas is highly desired. We are seeking a individual with excellent oral, written, interpersonal, and analytics skills who can thrive in a fast-paced environment. This position will directly impact the history of space exploration and will require your commitment and detailed attention towards safe and repeatable spaceflight.

                                                                                      

Responsibilities

  • Support business unit financial, operational, and strategic objectives
  • Develop and deliver content for strategic reviews with internal leadership
  • Source, aggregate and organize manufacturing content sourced through enterprise data warehouses or sourced through stakeholder input.  Design and push that content to display monitors throughout the campus.
  • Partner with support organizations to optimize the production ramp while meeting quality, cost, schedule requirements
  • Build and maintain tools, analytics, and reporting to highlight performance metrics and other key information
  • Assist with creation of models for financial reviews, cost benefit analysis, and metrics
  • Perform risk & opportunity analysis and provide potential solutions. Work with the team to develop recovery plans
  • Identify opportunities for capacity enhancement, factory/work-center efficiencies, production planning and supply chain optimization
  • Data mining across multiple data sets
  • Lead key business analytics initiatives, as required

Qualifications

  • 2+ years of business analysis or consulting experience
  • 2+ years of experience in a manufacturing and/or supply chain driven environment.
  • Experience working with Business Intelligence tools (e.g. Tableau)
  • Exceptional with Excel and manual data analysis
  • Proficiency with MS Office suite including MS Access
  • Excellent written communication and presentation skills
  • Excellent Communication Skills
  • Understanding of data structures and architecture
  • Aptitude for teamwork with demonstrated potential for leadership
  • Proven ability to work simultaneously on multiple projects

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst 3
Description:
Typical Day in the Role


Purpose of the Team: The main function of a Business Systems Analyst is to perform the analysis, design, implementation, modification, and daily functional support of all applications.
Key projects:


→ Works with key stakeholders within all business functions to align technology solutions with business strategies

→ Gathers requirements from business units and translate those to programmers and developers

→ Prepares cost-benefit and return-on-investment analyses to aid in decisions on system implementation

→ Demonstrates an informed knowledge of business functions to resolve problems and capitalize on improvement opportunities

→ Supports one or more highly complex business processes

→ Works on multiple projects as a project team member

→ Serves as a liaison between the business community and the IT organization in order to provide technical solutions to meet user needs


Best vs. Average:


→ Experience combining data analytics with supply chain operations (hybrid profile strongly preferred)

→ Strong data analysis and reporting skills, including ability to work with complex datasets

→ Ability to work in ambiguity and learn quickly in a fast‐moving environment

→Experience supporting supply chain operations (e.g., supply chain data, reporting, master data, or process analysis) in combination with data analytics. Hybrid data + supply chain background strongly preferred.

→ Clear communication skills; able to articulate prior work and analytical approach

Qualifications:


Bachelor's degree in a technical field such as computer science, computer engineering or related field required. MBA or other related advanced degree preferred
5-7 years experience required
Basic knowledge of programming languages in order to comprehend reading code and basic functions
Ability to create business solutions that increase competitive advantage
Ability to exercise good judgment in selecting methods and techniques for obtaining solutions
Project management skills in order to handle diverse projects, often times simultaneously, and meet aggressive deadlines
Ability to solve technical problems/ processes and understand complex details
Ability to increase operating efficiency with produce high quality technical solutions
A high level of interpersonal and verbal communication skills necessary to relate to other people at their systems knowledge level
Ability to analyze complex situations and problems and do the necessary research using multiple sources of information to arrive at innovative solutions


Explain a typical day in the role.:

Extracting data to support business decisions, building Power BIs, updating existing Power BIs, supporting Order fulfillment managers on daily tasks

What is the ideal background of a candidate for this role?: Supply Chain and Data science experience

What are unique selling points that would get candidates interested in your role over another?:

Supply Chain and Data science experience in the Cloud with a track record of driving improvement in previous space

How will contractor performance be measured?: Responsiveness, PBI delivery speed, meeting ETAs and proactive communication on risks to meeting ETAs

Top 3 Must-Have HARD Skills & years of experience for each:

Data analysis experience 3+ years

Supply Chain experience 3+ years

Microsoft office 3+ years

Title: Workforce Analytics Consultant
Description:
Join a team that shares your calling

At MultiCare, you’re more than just a job title — you’re part of a team built on trust that cares for each other, our patients and our communities. Belonging here means living our mission and values every day. If your purpose and passions align with ours, you’ll find a place to grow, do meaningful work and build a career you love in a community that feels like home.

FTE: 1.0, Shift: Days, Schedule: Exempt


Design, build, and maintain Workday reports, dashboards, and calculated fields to support business needs.
Develop and optimize automations within Workday to streamline processes and improve data accuracy.
Rebuild and maintain Workday Adaptive Planning sheets,
Create advanced analytics and data visualizations Workday Prism
Experience with Power BI and Workday Orchestrations preferred


Position Summary

The Workforce Analytics Consultant is responsible for the development and delivery of workforce analytics, scorecards/dashboards or reports to assist the organization with decisions regarding human capital management. This position is responsible for delivering data, both real-time as well as forecasted data that will assist with workforce planning and driving business outcomes by using data to influence decisions. A wide degree of creativity and latitude is expected in order to be successful in this position.

Requirements


Bachelor of Arts in Business Administration, Computer Science, Mathematics or related field; Master’s degree preferred
Minimum five (5) years’ experience in an analytical role
Experience with data analysis, data visualization, forecasting and modeling
Experience with SQL, or similar language preferred
Experience in Workforce Planning, Workforce Management, People Analytics or working with HR/organizational people data systems preferred
1 (one) year of experience in Workday Adaptive
Consideration may be given to internal candidates not meeting the minimum qualifications


Why MultiCare?

Rooted in the local community

Partnering with patients, families and neighbors across the Pacific Northwest for more than 140 years.

Growth and education

Competitive tuition assistance, award-winning residencies, fellowships and career development to invest in you.

Well-being and support

Generous PTO, Code Lavender and Employee Assistance Programs to help you maintain balance and feel cared in your work and life.

Living our values

Respect, integrity, kindness and collaboration guide how we care for patients, communities and each other.

Belonging for all

Resource Groups and outreach programs help ensure every team member feels safe, seen, heard and valued.

Pacific Northwest lifestyle

Work and live where natural beauty, adventure and strong community connections are part of everyday life.

Pay and Benefit Expectations

We provide a comprehensive benefits package, including competitive salary, medical, dental and retirement benefits and paid time off. As required by various pay transparency laws, we share a competitive range of compensation for candidates hired into each position. The pay scale is $127,171.20 - $175,614.40 USD. However, pay is influenced by factors specific to applicants, including but not limited to: skill set, level of experience, and certification(s) and/or education. If this position is associated with a union contract, pay will be reflective of the appropriate step on the pay scale to which the applicant’s years of experience align.

Associated benefit information can be viewed here.

Title: Data Analytics & BI with Tableau
Description:
Job Description

Must Have Technical/Functional Skills

We are seeking a skilled BI Analyst to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will design, build, and maintain scalable BI Solutions using Tableau, AWS data services to support our data-driven decision-making processes. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure data availability, integrity, and performance for analytics, machine learning, and business intelligence applications in an Agile environment.

Technical Skills


BI development experience using Tableau, AWS data services (Redshift, RDS, Athena, Glue).
Strong skills in Tableau for creating interactive dashboards and visualizations.
Ability to connect Tableau to various data sources and build real-time, data-driven insights.
Experience in designing user-friendly and visually appealing dashboards.
Expertise in SQL and Data Modeling and experience with relational databases (e.g., Redshift, RDS, Oracle, SAP HANA).


SQL Expertise


Advanced proficiency in SQL for querying, data manipulation, and optimization.
Ability to write complex SQL queries, including subqueries, joins, window functions, and CTEs.
Experience with database management systems like Microsoft SQL Server, Teradata or Oracle.
Performance tuning and optimization of SQL queries and stored procedures.
Familiarity with ETL processes and data integration.


Tableau Expertise


Strong skills in Tableau for creating interactive dashboards and visualizations.
Ability to connect Tableau to various data sources and build real-time, data-driven insights.
Proficiency in creating calculated fields, parameters, and leveraging Tableau's advanced visualization features.
Experience in designing user-friendly and visually appealing dashboards.


Supply Chain Domain Knowledge


Strong grasp of supply chain processes, including inventory management, procurement, and logistics.
Ability to define and track supply chain-specific KPIs and metrics.


Additional Skills


Basic understanding of data warehousing concepts.
Knowledge of scripting languages (e.g., Python or R) for data analysis is a plus.
Strong problem-solving and data interpretation abilities.
Excellent communication skills to convey complex data insights to stakeholders.


Roles & Responsibilities


Requirements gathering and stakeholder interviews to define Personas, Use cases, metrics, KPIs, and data sources.
Data profiling and assessment of data quality, completeness, and lineage for identified source systems.
Transformation logic creation (ETL/ELT) required to support dashboard metrics.
Development of interactive dashboards in Tableau with role-based views and filters.
Implementation of visualizations for agreed metrics and KPIs.
Basic performance tuning and optimization for dashboard load times.
Advanced proficiency in SQL for querying, data manipulation, and optimization.
In addition to having the knowledge required to perform the above activities:
Must have minimum 4 years of Tableau dashboard development.
Must have minimum 5 years of experience with complex data environments including advanced SQL and data modeling.
Track record of leading projects and training others.
Must be proficient in transforming complex data into actionable business insights and troubleshooting issues in analytics pipelines.
Consistently adopt best practices to ensure data accuracy and accountability.


Generic Managerial Skills, If any


Excellent problem solving, communications, interpersonal and analytical skills
Ability to understand and comprehend complex environments and systems
Inquisitive by nature and keen to figure out how things work


Base Salary Range : $120,000 to $140,000 Per Annum

TCS Employee Benefits Summary

Discretionary Annual Incentive.

Comprehensive Medical Coverage: Medical & Health, Dental & Vision, Disability Planning & Insurance, Pet Insurance Plans.

Family Support: Maternal & Parental Leaves.

Insurance Options: Auto & Home Insurance, Identity Theft Protection.

Convenience & Professional Growth: Commuter Benefits & Certification & Training Reimbursement.

Time Off: Vacation, Time Off, Sick Leave & Holidays.

Legal & Financial Assistance: Legal Assistance, 401K Plan, Performance Bonus, College Fund, Student Loan Refinancing.

Title: Product Sourcing Analyst
Description:
About Filson

In 1897 C.C. Filson began outfitting prospectors headed for the Gold Rush with tools and apparel strong enough for the toughest spots on earth. More than 100 years later, Filson remains committed to providing pioneers of all fields around the world with unfailing goods made from the highest quality materials available. Filson recognizes that it takes a world-class team to make a world-class product. As we build on the Filson legacy of American craftsmanship, we are looking for individuals who share our dedication to innovation, excellence, and superior customer service. We don’t work in silos but rely on open collaboration. It’s a jackknife mentality – the ability to accomplish more than what’s asked of you – that defines our culture of versatility and perseverance. Our outfitter mindset is deliberate and strategic. We are a small company, on purpose. We’re the ones doing the work, from product design and marketing, to cutting, sewing, and restoration. With more than 125 years under our belt, we still have a start-up mentality. Driven by an independent spirit and an uncompromising attitude that demands we lead with conviction.

About The Role

Reporting to Sourcing Manager, the Product Sourcing Analyst will play a key role in supporting the sourcing team through detailed data analysis, supplier performance evaluation, and process optimization. This position will focus on ensuring data-driven decision-making for Filson’s global and domestic sourcing strategies, helping to deliver high-quality products while maintaining cost efficiency and meeting delivery targets. This position is based at our corporate headquarters in Seattle.

As a Product Sourcing Analyst, you will collaborate closely with suppliers, design teams, product development, production, merchandising, and planning stakeholders to provide actionable insights that optimize sourcing strategies and support company goals.

Responsibilities


Analyze and support global and domestic sourcing strategies for all product categories
Collect, consolidate, and analyze data on supplier costs, lead times, and capacity to drive sourcing decisions
Prepare seasonal cost and margin reports, allocation breakdowns, and raw material analysis
Develop and maintain vendor and supplier scorecards, presenting findings to drive continuous improvement
Monitor and report on global market conditions affecting the supply chain, such as tariffs, freight challenges, and material costs
Support seasonal lead time tracking and coordinate with cross-functional teams to ensure timely product delivery
Contribute to process improvement initiatives, leveraging advanced Excel and data analysis skills
Perform other duties as assigned to support the overall sourcing and supply chain goals


Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in business, supply chain, or a related field; a minimum of 3–5 years of experience in a sourcing, production, or analytical role
Strong analytical skills with expertise in Excel and familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau)
Knowledge of global supply chain principles and market trends impacting sourcing and production
Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills
A strategic thinker with strong organizational and problem-solving abilities
Demonstrated ability to collaborate with internal teams and external suppliers
High sense of urgency, with the ability to work under tight deadlines and prioritize effectively
Experience with ERP or PLM systems is a plus


Physical Requirements

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met of any employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals to perform those essential functions.

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to:


Regularly sit and stand
Use hands, fingers, and arms to use computers and other office equipment
Use hands, fingers, and arms to lift, squeeze, pinch, grab products/items/supplies
Reach up, across, or down to retrieve products and supplies
See, talk, and hear
Occasionally required to move or lift up to 20 pounds


Note: This job description is not meant to be all-inclusive. Employee may be required to perform other duties to meet the ongoing needs of the organization.

It is our policy that all decisions involving any aspect of the employment relationship will be made without regard to race, color, sex, religion, age, national origin, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, the presence of any sensory, mental, or physical disability, veteran and military status, genetic information, political ideology or any other status or characteristic protected by local, state or federal law. About Filson

In 1897 C.C. Filson began outfitting prospectors headed for the Gold Rush with tools and apparel strong enough for the toughest spots on earth. More than 100 years later, Filson remains committed to providing pioneers of all fields around the world with unfailing goods made from the highest quality materials available. Filson recognizes that it takes a world-class team to make a world-class product. As we build on the Filson legacy of American craftsmanship, we are looking for individuals who share our dedication to innovation, excellence, and superior customer service. We don’t work in silos but rely on open collaboration. It’s a jackknife mentality – the ability to accomplish more than what’s asked of you – that defines our culture of versatility and perseverance. Our outfitter mindset is deliberate and strategic. We are a small company, on purpose. We’re the ones doing the work, from product design and marketing, to cutting, sewing, and restoration. With more than 125 years under our belt, we still have a start-up mentality. Driven by an independent spirit and an uncompromising attitude that demands we lead with conviction.

About The Role

Reporting to Sourcing Manager, the Product Sourcing Analyst will play a key role in supporting the sourcing team through detailed data analysis, supplier performance evaluation, and process optimization. This position will focus on ensuring data-driven decision-making for Filson’s global and domestic sourcing strategies, helping to deliver high-quality products while maintaining cost efficiency and meeting delivery targets. This position is based at our corporate headquarters in Seattle.

As a Product Sourcing Analyst, you will collaborate closely with suppliers, design teams, product development, production, merchandising, and planning stakeholders to provide actionable insights that optimize sourcing strategies and support company goals.

Responsibilities

  • Analyze and support global and domestic sourcing strategies for all product categories
  • Collect, consolidate, and analyze data on supplier costs, lead times, and capacity to drive sourcing decisions
  • Prepare seasonal cost and margin reports, allocation breakdowns, and raw material analysis
  • Develop and maintain vendor and supplier scorecards, presenting findings to drive continuous improvement
  • Monitor and report on global market conditions affecting the supply chain, such as tariffs, freight challenges, and material costs
  • Support seasonal lead time tracking and coordinate with cross-functional teams to ensure timely product delivery
  • Contribute to process improvement initiatives, leveraging advanced Excel and data analysis skills
  • Perform other duties as assigned to support the overall sourcing and supply chain goals

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in business, supply chain, or a related field; a minimum of 3–5 years of experience in a sourcing, production, or analytical role
  • Strong analytical skills with expertise in Excel and familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau)
  • Knowledge of global supply chain principles and market trends impacting sourcing and production
  • Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills
  • A strategic thinker with strong organizational and problem-solving abilities
  • Demonstrated ability to collaborate with internal teams and external suppliers
  • High sense of urgency, with the ability to work under tight deadlines and prioritize effectively
  • Experience with ERP or PLM systems is a plus

Physical Requirements

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met of any employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals to perform those essential functions.

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to:

  • Use hands, fingers, and arms to use computers and other office equipment
  • Use hands, fingers, and arms to lift, squeeze, pinch, grab products/items/supplies
  • Reach up, across, or down to retrieve products and supplies
  • See, talk, and hear
  • Occasionally required to move or lift up to 20 pounds

Title: Data Scientist - TikTok Ads
Description:
Responsibilities
About The TikTok Monetization Product Data Science Team
We're the TikTok Monetization Products data science team, who enables and champions data driven decision making. Our Vision is to become the world class data science team, where data is used rigorously to drive all decision making. Our Mission is to drive monetization and sustainable revenue growth for TikTok through data science.

Responsibilities：
1. Work with large, complex data sets. Solve difficult, non-routine analysis problems, applying advanced analytical methods as needed. Conduct analysis that includes data gathering and requirements specification, processing, analysis, ongoing deliverables, and presentations.
2. Build and prototype analysis pipelines iteratively to provide insights at scale.
3. Develop comprehensive knowledge of Tiktok data structures and metrics, advocating for changes where needed for product development.
Interact cross-functionally, making business recommendations (e.g., cost-benefit, forecasting, experiment analysis) with effective presentations of findings at multiple levels of stakeholders through visual displays of quantitative information.
4. Research and develop analysis, forecasting, and optimization methods to improve the quality of TikTok ads products.

Qualifications
Minimum Qualifications:
- Master's degree in a quantitative discipline (e.g., Statistics, Operations Research,
- Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics) or equivalent practical experience.
- Expertise with statistical data analysis such as linear models, multivariate analysis, stochastic models, sampling methods.
- Experience with statistical software (e.g. R, Python, MATLAB) and database languages (e.g. SQL)

Preferred Qualifications:
- Demonstrated leadership and self-direction. Willingness to both teach others and learn new techniques.
-Demonstrated skills in selecting the right statistical tools given a data analysis problem. Effective written and verbal communication skills.

About TikTok
TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​


Why Join Us
Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.​
We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​
​
Diversity & Inclusion​
TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​


TikTok Accommodation
TikTok is committed to providing reasonable accommodations in our recruitment processes for candidates with disabilities, pregnancy, sincerely held religious beliefs or other reasons protected by applicable laws. If you need assistance or a reasonable accommodation, please reach out to us at https://tinyurl.com/RA-request​


Job Information
【For Pay Transparency】Compensation Description (Annually)
The base salary range for this position in the selected city is $170240 - $277970 annually.​
Compensation may vary outside of this range depending on a number of factors, including a candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and experience, and location. Base pay is one part of the Total Package that is provided to compensate and recognize employees for their work, and this role may be eligible for additional discretionary bonuses/incentives, and restricted stock units.​
Benefits may vary depending on the nature of employment and the country work location. Employees have day one access to medical, dental, and vision insurance, a 401(k) savings plan with company match, paid parental leave, short-term and long-term disability coverage, life insurance, wellbeing benefits, among others. Employees also receive 10 paid holidays per year, 10 paid sick days per year and 17 days of Paid Personal Time (prorated upon hire with increasing accruals by tenure).​
The Company reserves the right to modify or change these benefits programs at any time, with or without notice.​
For Los Angeles County (unincorporated) Candidates:​
Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with all federal, state, and local laws including the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. Our company believes that criminal history may have a direct, adverse and negative relationship on the following job duties, potentially resulting in the withdrawal of the conditional offer of employment:​
1. Interacting and occasionally having unsupervised contact with internal/external clients and/or colleagues;​
2. Appropriately handling and managing confidential information including proprietary and trade secret information and access to information technology systems; and​
3. Exercising sound judgment.​
