Title: Senior Data Analyst- BigQuery and SQL Server
Description:
We get up every day, roll up our sleeves and build a better world -- together. At Ford, we’re all a part of something bigger than ourselves. Are you ready to change the way the world moves?

Ford of Canada's operations include a national headquarters, three vehicle assembly and engine manufacturing plants, three parts distribution centers, and three Connectivity and Innovation centers. Ford employs approximately 7,000 people in Canada, while an additional 18,000 people are employees in the more than 400 Ford and Ford-Lincoln dealerships across the country. For more information, please visit www.careers.ford.com

Enterprise Technology plays a critical part in shaping the future of mobility. If you’re looking for the chance to leverage advanced technology to redefine the transportation landscape, enhance the customer experience and improve people’s lives, this is the opportunity for you. Join us and challenge your IT expertise and analytical skills to help create vehicles that are as smart as you are.

What You'll Do...

The primary responsibilities for this position include but are not limited to:


Rapid Investigations & Stakeholder Support: Lead timely analysis for evolving, high-priority stakeholder questions; clarify problem statements, align on definitions, and deliver accurate findings with supporting evidence.
Time-Series & Sequence Analysis (BigQuery): Analyze time-ordered event/telemetry data at scale to establish context around key outcomes, identify precursor signals, and measure the frequency of specific event sequences.
Advanced SQL Development: Write efficient, well-structured SQL for analytical workflows, including heavy use of window functions for sequencing, sessionization, and “leading up to” / “following” event context.
Relational Data Analysis (SQL Server): Analyze non-time-series operational datasets in SQL Server; join across tables, validate definitions, and reconcile findings with event-based analytics where applicable.
Data Validation & Hypothesis Development: Assess data quality and result validity, identify anomalies, form and test hypotheses, and iterate analyses as new information emerges.
Multi-Source Data Integration: Integrate and reason across multiple data sources to provide a coherent view of what happened and under what conditions.
Log Pattern Tool Support (Rules & Execution): Work with stakeholders to define detection requirements, author and refine rule logic for our log-pattern analysis tool, execute investigations, and summarize findings along with recommended rule improvements.
Comprehensive Reporting: Deliver outputs ranging from concise executive summaries to detailed technical write-ups that document methods, assumptions, and contributing factors.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with engineering, product, and process-improvement stakeholders (including Six Sigma/Black Belt practitioners) to frame questions, define metrics, and support structured root-cause analysis efforts.
Executive Communication: Communicate complex findings calmly and clearly in high-visibility settings; distinguish evidence from hypotheses and avoid premature conclusions.


You'll have...

Education


Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Engineering, or a related quantitative field. (Successful candidates will be required to provide proof of degree completion for the highest level of education attained. If the degree was obtained from a school outside of Canada, an Education Credential Assessment report showing Canadian equivalency is also required).


Experience


Minimum 3 years experience in:
Data analysis, including time-series/event correlation and relational (non-time-series) analysis.
SQL, including expert use of analytical/window functions; with BigQuery and SQL Server (or equivalent relational database) experience.
Writing performance-conscious queries over large datasets (e.g., partitioning/clustering-aware approaches, minimizing unnecessary scans, and validating results efficiently).
Demonstrated ability to identify anomalies, validate assumptions, and use data to test hypotheses and refine problem statements.
Minimum 5 years of experience in visualization/reporting tools such as Power BI or Looker Studio.


Even better, you may have...

Education


Master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Engineering, or a related quantitative field (Successful candidates will be required to provide proof of degree completion for the highest level of education attained. If the degree was obtained from a school outside of Canada, an Education Credential Assessment report showing Canadian equivalency is also required).


Experience


Familiarity with structured problem-solving / continuous improvement approaches (e.g., Six Sigma, DMAIC), and comfort collaborating with Black Belt–level stakeholders.
Proficiency in Python (Pandas, NumPy) or R for analysis and workflow automation.
Knowledge of infotainment and vehicle systems, or experience in automotive data analysis.
Experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP) data services beyond BigQuery.
Experience with data transformation tools such as dbt.
Familiarity working with cloud infrastructure, DevOps, or embedded teams in cross-functional environments.
Effective written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to tailor explanations for both technical and non-technical audiences.
Ability to manage shifting priorities and deliver accurate work on tight timelines.


This posting is for an existing vacancy within our team.

The expected annual salary range for this Ford of Canada position is $85,000.00 - $135,000.00 and individuals may be eligible to participate in our Annual Compensation bonus program.

Our focus is to build the best team. If you think you can bring value to Ford, love to collaborate, prioritize and aim to deliver excellence in everything you do, we encourage you to apply!

Successful candidates will be required to provide proof of degree completion for the highest level of education attained. If the degree was obtained from a school outside of Canada, an Education Credential Assessment report showing Canadian equivalency is also required.

We thank all candidates for their interest, but only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

Ford of Canada is an Equal Opportunity Employer and is committed to a culturally diverse workforce.

Accommodations for applicants with disabilities throughout the recruitment, selection and/or assessment processes, where needed, are available upon request. Please inform Human Resources of the nature of any accommodation(s) that you may require.

Candidates for this position must be legally entitled to work in Canada. Ford Motor Company of Canada, Limited, does not sponsor work permit applications.

This position is hybrid. Candidates who are in commuting distance to a Ford hub location may be required to be onsite four or more days per week.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Who We Are

Konrad is a next-generation digital consultancy. We are dedicated to solving complex business problems for our global clients with creative and forward-thinking solutions. Our employees enjoy a culture built on innovation and a commitment to creating best-in-class digital products in use by hundreds of millions of consumers around the world. We hire exceptionally smart, analytical, and hard-working people who are lifelong learners.

About The Role

This role sits on our Digital team, where your internal title will be Specialist, Digital, working primarily in a data analytics capacity across client engagements.

As a Specialist, Digital focused on Data Analytics, you are an early-to-mid career data professional with a strong foundation in analysis and a desire to grow both your technical and consulting skill set. Leveraging your analytics background, you will support the delivery of digital products for our clients through analytics and reporting solutions that help organizations better understand their data and make informed decisions.

In addition to your analytics work, you will gain exposure to broader areas of digital consulting, including digital product development, digital transformation, and user experience (UX/CX). You are interested in understanding how data supports product decisions, customer experiences, and overall business strategy.

You will work closely with senior analysts, data scientists, engineers, product managers, designers, and business stakeholders across a variety of client engagements. With guidance and mentorship, you will contribute to analytics initiatives including dashboarding, exploratory analysis, KPI tracking, and data quality validation, while also building broader consulting skills across areas such as project management, user experience, and design thinking.

You are curious, detail-oriented, and motivated to continuously improve your analytical and consulting capabilities.

What You’ll Do


Support client and internal teams in understanding business questions and translating them into analytical requirements
Analyze data to identify trends, patterns, and insights under the guidance of senior team members Build and maintain dashboards and recurring reports to track key metrics and performance indicators
Assist in documenting metrics, definitions, and analytics processes
Collaborate with data engineers and other analysts to support data modeling and reporting layers Communicate insights clearly through visualizations, summaries, and presentations
Proactively identify opportunities to improve reporting, data quality, and analytical workflows


Qualifications


Minimum 2+ years of experience in a data analyst, business intelligence, or analytics-focused role
Programming experience in Python, Bash scripting, R or related language
Working proficiency in SQL and experience querying cloud data warehouses (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Redshift, BigQuery)
Experience with Business Intelligence and data visualization tools (e.g., Looker, Tableau, Power BI)
Foundational understanding of data modeling and analytics best practices
Experience defining and tracking KPIs and metrics
A/B testing and causal analysis
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail
Clear written and verbal communication skills Ability to work collaboratively in cross-functional and consulting-style environments
Interest in learning about data governance, privacy, and security best practices


Perks And Benefits


Comprehensive Health & Wellness Benefits Package
Retirement Planning
Parental Leave Program
Flexible Working Hours
Work from Home Flexibility
Service Recognition Programs
Socials, Outings & Retreats
Culture of Learning & Development


Bonus Points

Have you taken any courses at BrainStation? A lot of our design and development best practices and processes are taught during our courses, make sure to highlight this experience in your cover letter if you have!

Konrad is committed to maintaining a diverse work environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status will receive consideration for employment. If you have any accessibility requirements or concerns regarding the hiring process or employment with us, please notify us so we can provide suitable accommodation.

The estimated compensation for this position is $75,000 to $100,000. This is an estimate and a compensation offer will vary based on applicant’s education, experience, skills, abilities and alignment with market data.

While we sincerely appreciate all applications, only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Number: R0238065

Data Analyst

The Opportunity:

As a data analyst, you love diving into data and turning it into meaningful insights. With the abundance of structured and unstructured data, you understand the importance of transforming complex data sets into useful information to solve challenges. As a data analyst at Booz Allen, you can use your skills and experience to support a mission and use data for good. We need a data expert like you to bring your expertise to empower our clients to understand their data.

As a client-facing data analyst with the U.S. Army, you’ll work closely with your clients to understand their data questions and then dig into their data-rich environments and tools to find the pieces of their information puzzle. Not only will you provide a deep understanding of their data, but you’ll also advise your client on what the information means by developing decision-making support visualization products and dashboards to help translate data into recommendations. You will use artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) to automate the analysis of large amounts of information environment content in real time.

Work with us to drive large-scale business and process decisions through data insights.

What You'll Work On:


Design and shape data pipelines to analyze the information environment in a streamlined way to provide sentient and stance analysis, trend forecasting, and instant crisis alerts.
Research, develop, and test data methodologies, and generate cross-functional solutions through collection, interpretation, evaluation, and analysis of large data sets.
Contribute to impactful work and guide decision-making across multiple organizations.
Establish quantitative and qualitative metrics and key performance indicators to drive technical outcomes.
Apply data visualization through different formats such as graphs, tables, and maps on various platforms.



Join us. The world can’t wait.

You Have:


3+ years of experience with data analysis, data science, and data visualization, including integrating data feeds to display information
Experience with AI, ML, and data management
Experience with quantitative data analysis, performance measures, data cleansing, metrics analysis, and applied research
Experience leveraging various programs to facilitate process automation while maintaining data integrity
Ability to translate complex and technical findings into an easily understood narrative in graphical, verbal, or written formats for non-technical audiences or executive leadership
Ability to execute projects and tasks with minimal guidance and supervision 
Ability to obtain a Secret clearance
Bachelor's degree in Data Science



Nice If You Have:


2+ years of experience identifying and collecting data using various tools and methods, including Palantir Foundry or Maven Smart Systems
Experience working with data in Department of War Information Environments
Experience in data engineering and programming languages such as R, Python, or SQL
Experience with social media monitoring tools such as Talkwalker, Sprinklr, or Meltwater
Experience analyzing operational assessment methodologies and decision criteria and preparing and presenting briefings to senior leadership
Experience analyzing data in the Indo Pacific theater of operations
Possession of excellent verbal and written communication skills
Master's degree in Data Science



Clearance:

Applicants selected will be subject to a security investigation and may need to meet eligibility requirements for access to classified information.

Compensation

At Booz Allen, we celebrate your contributions, provide you with opportunities and choices, and support your total well-being. Our offerings include health, life, disability, financial, and retirement benefits, as well as paid leave, professional development, tuition assistance, work-life programs, and dependent care. Our recognition awards program acknowledges employees for exceptional performance and superior demonstration of our values. Full-time and part-time employees working at least 20 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in Booz Allen’s benefit programs. Individuals that do not meet the threshold are only eligible for select offerings, not inclusive of health benefits. We encourage you to learn more about our total benefits by visiting the Resource page on our Careers site and reviewing Our Employee Benefits page.

Salary at Booz Allen is determined by various factors, including but not limited to location, the individual’s particular combination of education, knowledge, skills, competencies, and experience, as well as contract-specific affordability and organizational requirements. The projected compensation range for this position is $77,600.00 to $176,000.00 (annualized USD). The estimate displayed represents the typical salary range for this position and is just one component of Booz Allen’s total compensation package for employees. This posting will close within 90 days from the Posting Date.

Identity Statement

As part of the hiring process, we will ask you to complete an identity verification process that leverages advanced biometrics and artificial intelligence to ensure authenticity and protect against identity fraud. You are expected to be on camera during interviews and assessments. We reserve the right to take your picture to verify your identity and prevent fraud.

Candidate AI Usage Policy

AI is a part of our daily work at Booz Allen, and we are committed to the responsible and ethical use of AI tools. However, we want to ensure a fair candidate process based on your own skills and knowledge. As part of this commitment, the use of artificial intelligence (AI) or other tools to assist with responses during interviews (whether in-person or virtual) is prohibited unless permission is explicitly provided.

Work Model

Our people-first culture prioritizes the benefits of collaboration, whether it occurs in person or virtually. To support engagement and effective communication, employees working virtually are generally expected to have their cameras on during meetings.


Remote: If this position is listed as remote, there may still be occasions when you are required to work in person at a Booz Allen or customer facility.
Hybrid: If this position is listed as hybrid, you will be expected to work from a Booz Allen facility frequently, in alignment with leadership expectations and the needs of the role. You may also be required to work from or visit a customer facility.
Onsite: If this position is listed as onsite, work will primarily be performed at a Booz Allen office or customer facility, where employees will collaborate directly with colleagues and customers as required by the role.



Commitment to Non-Discrimination

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to disability, status as a protected veteran or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, local, or international law.

Title: Category Management Analyst
Description:
Welcome Home!

We’re more than Canada’s largest independent hardware and building supplies retailer—we’re part of communities across the nation.




Data-Driven Category Management

As the Category Management Analyst, you will support the Merchandise teams with the development and execution of detailed category management plans. Led by Manager, Category Management, Planning and Programs, this opportunity will best suit someone who has strong analytical and organizational skills.




*Please note this is a 12-month term position. Ready to make an impact in a new role? Here’s how:

Gather and input data; build, analyze and distribute reports and Dealer surveys to strengthen merchandise decisions and meet financial goals and objectives. Perform quantitative and qualitative analysis of internal data including sales, inventory, and pricing data, and external industry, market, channel, and economic data.
Schedule and coordinate all activities related to the line review/negotiation process. Including key meetings, survey schedules, and category/negotiation launch schedules.
Support the accountability of the LBM negotiations and HL Category reviews line commitments through meeting with the specific merchant and cross functional teams at key points in the Gantt chart to ensure negotiations/reviews are on track. Report any delays or risks to schedule to the Manager of Category Management and appropriate Merchandise Director. Provide any relevant updates in the weekly cross functional update meeting.
Build knowledge of category management processes; understand key stakeholders both internally and externally and their involvement in the process.
Provide process support with activities including but not limited to Imports, Private brands, Quality Management and After-Sales service.
Assist with tactical insight recommendations to capitalize on opportunities to improve category performance.
Collaborate with the Merchandise Managers to populate standardized category templates supporting the ongoing line reviews and annual category management reviews to help drive actionable insights.




We’re looking for:

Diploma in Business, Math or Data Analytics and 3-5 years of experience.
Strong analytical and process skills.
Organizational agility, strategic acumen and comfort with ambiguity.
Analytical and computer skills and be familiar with Excel, Access and Microsoft Power BI.
The successful candidate will have comfort with project and process management.
Strong interpersonal skills are required, including being able to effectively communicate and build strong relationships with stakeholders.
Category management/business analysis, synthesize information to develop understanding and insights
The ability to be onsite three days a week at our corporate office in St. Jacobs, to accommodate our hybrid working model.




How we’ll make you feel at Home

Our Team Members enjoy life at Home with our strong culture of support and inclusion, excellent benefits, and a team that always has your back. You will receive:

The expected starting salary range for this role is $63,400- $83,300; the final offer will reflect relevant skills and experience.
Comprehensive Benefits Program including:
Health, Dental, Vision, Paramedical, Disability coverage, Out of Country Travel Insurance, Life Insurance, Employee Family Assistance Program.
Group RRSP.
Discount at our Corporate Retail Store in St. Jacobs.
Home & Auto Insurance discounts.
Competitive vacation time.
Employee Discount Program through Venngo WorkPerks.




This job posting is for an existing vacancy within our organization. Bring your unique skills, thrive with interesting and challenging work, and experience what it’s like at Home.




Apply today - this is a great opportunity to join a proudly Canadian company that is 100% ready to help you build your future.




Home Hardware recognizes the value of a diverse team. We believe that different perspectives, backgrounds, and experiences make us stronger as an organization. Applicants representing the full diversity of communities in Canada are encouraged to apply with confidence. Please let us know if you require accommodation during the recruitment process.




Canadians call us Home Hardware. We call it Home.

Description:

Job Description Summary

We are seeking an Analyst, Business Intelligence to support the design, development, and maintenance of interactive dashboards and reports for Inventory Management stakeholders. This role will work closely with Inventory Management, Accounting, FP&A, and business teams to translate financial data into meaningful insights that support decision-making.

The ideal candidate has strong experience in Microsoft Power BI, solid understanding of Inventory Management processes, and the ability to transform complex datasets into intuitive visualizations.

Responsibilities

Job Description


Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports for Inventory Management processes (e.g., inventory health, stock levels, turns, aging, service levels, fill rate, OTIF, and KPI tracking).
Collaborate with Inventory Management, Demand Planning, and Purchasing stakeholders to gather business requirements and translate them into scalable reporting and analytics solutions.
Build and optimize Power BI data models, measures, and relationships to support inventory visibility and decision-making.
Develop and maintain ETL processes using Power Query and related data transformation tools to integrate inventory, supply, and demand data.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across inventory reports and analytics outputs.
Manage and maintain Power BI workspaces, including access control, deployment pipelines, and governance practices.
Leverage Microsoft Fabric capabilities (e.g., dataflows, lakehouse, semantic models) to enhance data integration, scalability, and performance of inventory analytics.
Work with data from ERP systems (e.g., SAP), warehouse management systems, and other enterprise data sources.
Optimize report performance and troubleshoot issues related to data refresh, model performance, and visualization efficiency.
Document data models, report logic, KPIs, and business definitions related to inventory management.
Provide training and support to end-users on Power BI dashboards and self-service inventory analytics capabilities.
Support continuous improvement initiatives focused on inventory optimization, working capital reduction, and service level performance.
Leverage Python for advanced data analysis, automation, and data transformation (e.g., building data pipelines, performing complex calculations, and enhancing Power BI datasets through integrated workflows).


Qualifications


3–5 years of experience in BI/reporting roles, with at least 2–3 years of hands-on experience in Power BI.
Strong proficiency in:
Power BI Desktop & Service
Power Query (M language)
Data modeling concepts
Solid understanding of Inventory Management and accounting principles.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with relational databases.
Experience integrating data from ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Dynamics, etc.) is an asset.
Hands-on experience with Power BI Service, including workspace management and deployment pipelines.
Familiarity with Microsoft Fabric (Dataflows Gen2, Lakehouse, or related components) is an asset.
Ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.

The Anticipated Salary Range For This Position

$81,600.00 - $122,400.00 Annual

This job description is not intended to be all-inclusive. Employee may perform other related duties as required to meet the ongoing needs of the organization.

Medline Canada, Corporation is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.

About Medline Canada

Medline Canada stands at the forefront of healthcare, providing expertise and a breadth of solutions for every care setting. With 550+ employees, including 100 dedicated sales and clinical professionals and eight distribution centres located coast-to-coast, Medline Canada is proud to serve the entire continuum of care.

Medline Canada is committed to diversity and inclusivity and we encourage applications from members of groups that have been historically disadvantaged and marginalized, including First Nations, Metis and Inuit peoples, Indigenous peoples of North America, racialized persons/persons of colour and those who identify as women, persons with disabilities and LGBTQ+ persons.

Medline Canada is committed to the principles of the accessibility in Canada. If you require any accommodations at any point during the application and hiring process, please contact HRCanada@medline.com.

All qualified candidates are encouraged to apply; however, Canadians and permanent residents will be given priority.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
About Pacific Blue Cross

Pacific Blue Cross (PBC) has been British Columbia's leading benefits provider for over 80 years. We are an independent, not-for-profit society with strong roots in BC’s health care system. We provide health, dental, life, disability, and travel coverage to 1 in 3 British Columbians through employee group plans and individual plans.

We are fueled by a commitment to keep health care sustainable for all British Columbians. Through our PBC Health Foundation, we fund projects that improve health outcomes directly related to mental health and wellbeing as well as the prevention and/or management of chronic disease. We are interested in finding people who want to make a difference and who are looking to grow their career with us.

We offer an attractive compensation and benefits package. Our workplace culture values health and wellness, diversity and equality, continuing education, environmental sustainability and giving back to the community.

Perks


Flexible working hours of 7.5 hours per day, Monday to Friday (i.e., 37.5 hours per week).
Paid vacation starts at 4 weeks per year, and increases with years of service.
Hybrid work environment (i.e., a combination of work from office and work from home days).
Generous benefits, including extended health, dental, and life insurance; depending on the plan that you choose, these benefit premiums can be 100% paid by PBC.
PBC contributes the equivalent of 8% of your base salary to a Defined Contribution pension plan. No employee contribution or matching is required, giving you more take-home pay.
Retiree Benefits plan that is available to employees who have at least 10 years of service with PBC and have reached the age of 55.
Onsite gym, cafeteria, and access to virtual doctors/counsellors 24/7 via our Employee Family Assistance Program!


About The Position


We are searching for a permanent Senior Data Analyst to join our Strategy and Analytics team.
PBC’s compensation program is built on a pay-for-performance philosophy, rewarding both individual and organizational achievements. The typical hiring range for this position is $100,000 - $120,000 per year, with the starting salary determined based on a combination of skills, education, experience, and internal equity. As part of our total rewards package, you’ll be eligible for our annual bonus program.


Imagine stepping into a role where your work turns complex data into clarity that leaders can use to make decisions.

Here, you are not just building reports, you are helping shape how the business understands itself. You work closely with stakeholders to take messy, complex datasets and turn them into meaningful insights that drive strategy, improve performance, and support better decisions across the organization.

This role is ideal for someone with experience as a Data Analyst, Senior BI Analyst, Senior Business Intelligence Analyst, BI Developer, or Analytics Developer, with strong hands-on skills in Python, SQL, and Power BI.

You are naturally analytical, resourceful, and comfortable working in ambiguity. You enjoy solving problems without clear instructions and can work independently while staying connected to the bigger business picture. You don’t just analyze data you understand what the business is trying to achieve and translate information into something actionable.

You are also energized by innovation and emerging technologies, including AI and modern analytics tools, and you are always looking for better, smarter ways to solve problems. Most importantly, you know that insights only matter when they are understood and used so you focus on clear, simple, and impactful storytelling.

If this sounds like the kind of work you want to do, we would love to hear from you. Apply today and bring your expertise in data, analytics, and storytelling to a role where your insights will directly influence decisions, shape strategy, and help the organization see the business more clearly.

We offer an attractive compensation and benefits package. Our workplace culture values health and wellness, diversity and equality, continuing education, environmental sustainability and giving back to the community.

This role is currently working hybrid from home and from our head office in Burnaby, BC. Apply this week if you would like to take on this role.

Key Ways This Position Makes An Impact

In this role, you sit at the center of decision making, where data, business understanding, and strategy all come together.

You start with large, complex datasets and bring structure to them using Python and SQL cleaning, transforming, and exploring the data to uncover trends, risks, and opportunities that are not immediately visible.

From there, your work shifts from technical analysis to meaning. You translate complex findings into clear, business friendly insights, turning analysis into dashboards, reports, and narratives that help leaders quickly understand what is happening and what actions are needed.

At the same time, you play a key role in strengthening how data is used across the organization. Working with databases, Business Intelligence (Power BI) tools, and data integration systems, you help ensure information is reliable, consistent, and easy to access so teams can trust and rely on it.

Over time, your contributions help mature the organization’s analytics capability, reducing reliance on ad hoc reporting and enabling more standardized, scalable, and trusted insights that support better decision-making.

You also help move the organization forward by supporting predictive thinking and engaging with AI-enabled and modern analytics tools, helping leaders shift from reactive decisions to more proactive, forward-looking planning.

Ultimately, your work makes a simple but powerful difference: leaders see the business more clearly, understand it more deeply, and make better decisions with confidence, backed by trusted data.

Key Experiences You Bring To This Role


Bachelor’s degree in data science, statistics, mathematics, or a related quantitative field.
5+ years of related experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or data science.
Proficiency with SQL, Python, and/or R for data manipulation and statistical analysis.
Proficiency with modern business intelligence tools such as Power BI (preferred), Tableau, and/or Looker for data visualization.
Demonstrated ability to analyze large datasets and translate findings into meaningful recommendations.
Strong critical thinking, problem solving, and analytical skills.
Excellent time management and organizational abilities, with capacity to balance competing priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Strong interpersonal and communication skills with a proven ability to collaborate effectively across teams and build relationships with stakeholders.


If you’re excited about taking on this opportunity, we invite you to apply today and start the conversation with us about this position.

Please apply via our website this week at https://www.pac.bluecross.ca/careers

While we thank all applicants for their interest, only short-listed candidates will be contacted.

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

PBC is an Indigenous Works employer of choice, an Insurance Business Canada Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion award winner, one of Canada’s Greenest employers, a Canadian Centre for Diversity and Inclusion (CCDI) employer partner, and a Pride at Work partner.

We strive to create a workplace where everyone feels valued and employees feel empowered to freely participate and contribute regardless of race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, religion, ability, education level, parental status, or socioeconomic status. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applications from all qualified candidates.

To request an accommodation in completing this job application, testing, interviewing, or otherwise participating in the employee selection process, please direct your inquiries to careers@pac.bluecross.ca. These accommodation requests will be reviewed confidentially and, on a case-by-case basis by Human Resources.

Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
Publitalia '80, concessionaria di pubblicità delle reti televisive free del Gruppo Mediaset, occupa una posizione di leadership nel mercato nazionale ed europeo, è alla ricerca di una figura da inserire in qualità di Data Analyst che supporterà la Direzione Marketing




La risorsa selezionata si occuperà:




Di raccogliere, pulire e organizzare grandi dataset provenienti da varie fonti
Di sviluppare modelli econometrici
Di generare report chiari e concisi che riassumano i risultati dell'analisi e forniscano raccomandazioni basate sui dati.




Profilo

Laurea in Economia/Ingegneria gestionale/Statistica/Matematica o affini

Title: Data Analyst - Alternance
Description:
Référence 26000EOM

Vos missions au quotidien

Basé dans nos locaux à Boulogne-Billancourt ( 92 ) au sein de la Direction de la direction Marketing .Vous aurez pour mission de développer de nouveaux outils (dashboards Tableau et modèles de machine learning) afin d’apporter de la connaissance, et faciliter le pilotage de l’activité Marketing Epargne.

Dans le cadre de vos missions, vous serez amené à :


Concevoir et assurer la maintenance de dashboards de pilotage de l’activité commerciale et marketing (via Tableau Software)
Réalisation d’études statistiques et des analyses de connaissance clie
Construction de modèle de machine learning (segmentation, scoring, prévision)
Participer activement aux réflexions sur l’optimisation des processus, des architectures et des pratiques de développement


Et si c’était vous ?

Vous recherchez une alternance d’une durée d’un an à partir de août 2026 dans le cadre d’un Bac+5 en statistique, data ou économétrie , science de la donnée, exploration et modélisation statistique, datamining, big data. Vous avez déjà eu une première expérience dans la Data à travers un stage, un projet scolaire ou un mémoire.

Vous maîtrisez SQL, SAS, Python ainsi que les outils de datavisualisation (Power BI, Tableau, Looker…) et PowerPoint. Vous avez également des notions en bourse et épargne..

Vous êtes reconnu(e) pour votre proactivité, votre écoute active, votre capacité d’analyse et votre enthousiasme

Alors rejoignez-nous ! Si votre candidature est sélectionnée, vous serez invité(e) à réaliser une vidéo de présentation. Nous vous contacterons ensuite pour un entretien.

Durée de l'alternance : 1 an

Date de début : Août 2026

Lieu : Boulogne-Billancourt (92)

Pourquoi nous choisir ?

BoursoBank, filiale du groupe Société Générale, est pionnière et leader sur ses deux activités : la banque en ligne et l’information économique et financière sur Internet avec son Portail Boursorama.com.

Avec son positionnement unique de banque-média et un développement centré sur les nouvelles attentes des clients (autonomie, simplicité, accès à l’information, sécurité et frais réduits), BoursoBank compte aujourd’hui plus 8 millions de clients et conforte, d’année en année, sa position d’acteur bancaire majeur en France.

Engagée à simplifier la banque, à donner du pouvoir d’achat à ses clients et à offrir à chacun la faculté d’agir sur son argent, BoursoBank a aussi l’ambition de participer de façon active et responsable à la transition écologique de ses clients en plus de celle qu’elle opère pour son propre fonctionnement. C’est grâce et avec l’ensemble de ses équipes, sensibilisées et actives à l’optimisation de l’impact environnemental et social quel que soit leur métier, que BoursoBank est certifiée B Corp depuis février 2023 et labélisée Numérique Responsable de niveau 2 depuis mars 2025.

BoursoBank a également à cœur d’assurer le bien-être de ses équipes en proposant divers avantages à travers notamment un accord d’intéressement motivant, un plan d’Epargne Entreprise, un plan d’épargne Retraite et une prise en charge à plus de 60 % de la mutuelle et des tickets restaurant.

Et parce que la diversité est une force, BoursoBank s’engage à offrir des opportunités inclusives et accueillantes pour tous.

Envie de prendre part à cette aventure professionnelle enrichissante et construire un projet professionnel solide ?

Rejoignez vos 1 000 futurs collègues dans nos locaux de Boulogne-Billancourt (92) ou, pour certains de nos métiers, de Nancy (54), pour faire partie d’une équipe où les talents de chacun sont développés !
Show more

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Descripción oferta

Desde la Dirección de NEGOCIO, precisamos incorporar un/a DATA ANALYST en ANÁLISIS DE NEGOCIO, ubicado en BARCELONA.

Su misión será facilitar a los equipos de Negocio información de valor sobre la evolución del negocio y los clientes de VidaCaixa, facilitando la obtención de insights y conclusiones a partir de la explotación analítica de datos. La persona que se incorpore combinará el conocimiento de técnicas y herramientas de explotación de datos con fuertes capacidades analíticas y conocimiento de negocio.

¿Cuál será tu propósito dentro de VidaCaixa?


Desarrollo y divulgación de análisis e informes que permitan comprender la evolución del negocio y faciliten la obtención de insights.
Análisis en profundidad que faciliten la toma de decisiones y acompañen la estrategia de negocio: evolución de las ventas y las cancelaciones por producto, impacto de campañas, lanzamiento de nuevos productos, simuladores y creación de escenarios, valoración de oportunidades y potencial de negocio, …
Incorporación de la visión cliente que permita entender las necesidades y comportamientos de los clientes a partir la analítica avanzada de datos.
Desarrollo de cuadros de mando (QlikSense) y procesos automatizados que permitan innovar y optimizar la explotación y acceso transversal a los datos.
Innovación y mejora continua en las formas de explotar y visualizar la información, explorando el potencial de nuevas herramientas y la IA.
Trabajar juntamente con los equipos de negocio para la identificación de nuevas oportunidades a partir de los datos.
Conocimiento de las bases de datos y el sistema informacional a partir del que se explotan los datos de clientes.



Requisitos:

Eres el talento que estamos buscando si cuentas con…


Formación universitaria superior (Matemáticas, Estadística, Ingeniería, Informática, Física…) o cualquier formación con foco en especialidad analítica.
Nivel avanzado en programación SQL para el análisis y transformación de bases de datos.
Experiencia previa como Data Analyst o analista de Business Intelligence: análisis de perfil de clientes, seguimientos de negocio, definición de indicadores, etc.
Eres una persona con iniciativa, organizada y orientada a resultados.
Cuentas con capacidad analítica para la extracción, comunicación y presentación de conclusiones a los responsables de negocio a partir de los datos.
Facilidad para trabajar en equipo y en proyectos con equipos transversales.
Curiosidad, predisposición a la innovación, aprendizaje y mejora continua.
Se valorará de forma positiva experiencia en herramientas de diseño de cuadros de mando y visualización de datos (QlikSense, PowerBI, Tableau).
Se valorará de forma positiva experiencia previa en el sector bancario o asegurador.



Beneficios

Sabemos que harás un gran trabajo, por eso hemos pensado en estos beneficios para ti…


Contrato indefinido
Flexibilidad horaria de entrada (7:30 a 9:30) y en la comida (puedes emplear entre un mínimo de 45 minutos y un máximo de 2 horas) para que puedas autogestionar tu tiempo
Plan de acogida y seguimiento de tu desarrollo profesional, con evaluaciones 360 y planes de desarrollo individuales.
Acceso a una plataforma online de formación, con un amplio catálogo autoformativo, para que tu aprendizaje sea continuo.
Todos los viernes con jornada intensiva para que puedas disfrutar más del fin de semana.
Del 15 de junio al 15 de septiembre jornada intensiva TODOS los días, porque sabemos que es verano y quieres disfrutar más tiempo de las horas de sol
Modelo de trabajo híbrido. Porque creemos que pasar tiempo en la oficina es un factor clave para desarrollar la creatividad, y disfrutar en equipo.
Fruta, porque es importante cuidarse. ¡Mens sana, in corpore sano!
29 días hábiles de vacaciones. ¡Desconectar, para volver a conectar!
Plan de Beneficios: plan de pensiones, seguro de salud, seguro de vida, ticket restaurante
Retribución flexible aplicado a guardería y/o transporte.



Proceso

¿Cómo será el proceso de selección?


Recibimos y validamos tu solicitud
Evaluación telefónica con especialista de selección
Realización de informe
Entrevista con el/la responsable de departamento
Entrevista personal con HRBP
¡Y no lo olvides, te daremos feedback!

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

YOUR MISSION

You will be at the heart of Glovo's Global Logistics and Operations team, owning the data work behind how we design, test, and optimize the order compensation structures that reward our riders across 23 countries. This is one of the highest impact areas in the company, rider payments are our largest operational cost, and every model you build or experiment you run translates directly into business outcomes at scale.

The environment is fast moving. Priorities shift and ad hoc requests come in regularly. This internship is built for someone curious, hands-on, and eager to learn how real operational decisions get made with data. You will be paired with experienced analysts who will mentor you along the way.

THE JOURNEY


Support the team in modelling and reviewing order payment structures that improve engagement and operational efficiency.
You will be using SQL to query large datasets to surface trends, answer business questions quickly, and build the analytical foundation for strategic decisions.
Translate findings into action. You will help present insights to non-technical stakeholders in a way that is concise and convincing.
Support the design and running of experiments that validate setting changes before global rollout. You will be exposed to the full cycle: design, execution, and analysis.
Leverage AI in your workflow. You will actively incorporate AI tools (agents, copilots, and LLM-assisted analysis) into your day to day work to move faster and work smarter.
Monitor and iterate. Track KPIs, spot anomalies, and make data driven adjustments on an ongoing basis.
Understand the rider perspective to ensure models are fair, locally relevant, and operationally sound.


Qualifications

WHAT YOU WILL BRING TO THE RIDE


Recently completed a degree or master's in Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, or another quantitative field.
SQL is essential. You can write queries to pull and filter data, join tables, and aggregate results on your own.
Python familiarity. You can use pandas (or similar) to manipulate data and automate simple tasks.
Excel / spreadsheet familiarity. You know your way around formulas, pivot tables, and structuring data cleanly.
Strong problem-solving instincts. You can take an ambiguous question, break it down, and work toward a clear answer without waiting to be told exactly how.
Fluent English is a must. You communicate clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.


Nice-to-haves


Hands-on experience leveraging AI in your workflow: copilots, agents, or LLM-assisted analysis used to move faster and work smarter.
Exposure to data visualization tools (Looker, Tableau, Power BI).
Any exposure to experimentation (A/B testing, switchback tests, or similar).


We Believe Driven Talent Deserves


🍔 Monthly Glovo credit to satisfy your cravings!
💳 Discounts on transportation, food, and even kindergarten expenses.
🏊 Discounted gym memberships to keep you energized.
🏖️ Extra time off, the freedom to work from home two days a week, and the opportunity to work from anywhere for up to three weeks a year!
👪 Enhanced parental leave, and office-based nursery.
🧠 Online therapy and wellbeing benefits to ensure your mental well-being.


Here at Glovo, we thrive on diversity, we believe it enhances our teams, products, and culture. We know that the best ideas come from a mashup of brilliant diverse minds. This is why we are committed to providing equal opportunities to talent from all backgrounds - all genders, racial/diverse backgrounds, abilities, ages, sexual orientations and all other unique characteristics that make you YOU. We will encourage you to bring your authentic self to work, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels heard.

Feel free to note your pronouns in your application (e.g., she/her/hers, he/him/his, they/them/theirs, etc).

So, ready to take the wheel and make this the ride of your life?

Additional Information

At Glovo, your success is defined by both results and behaviors. We hire for excellence in craft and for the Leadership Principles that shape how we think, decide, and collaborate. What you achieve matters and how you achieve it defines us. Together, they move the business forward and deliver great experiences. Learn more about our Leadership Principles here.

Title: Data Analyst Junior
Description:
VINCLE, consultora tecnológica en expansión, busca incorporar para un importante proyecto con gran multinacional de gran consumo, un Data Analyst Junior.




Te incorporarás a uno de los proyectos más pioneros del mercado, basado en la automatización de la demanda y de los suministros. Centrado en elaborar la previsión de la demanda y asegurar que el nivel de stock es el que se establece desde la dirección del departamento. Todo centralizado en departamentos de Supply y Demand.




🚀 FUNCIONES PRINCIPALES:

- Responsable del seguimiento y análisis de los KPI's clave del proyecto: Forecast Accuracy, Cambios, OSA, Freshness, Ventas.

- Interlocución con los departamentos de sistemas y gestión de incidencias relacionadas con el Forecast como principal input de la generación de los pedidos.

- Mantenimiento de los parámetros necesarios para el correcto dimensionamiento de los pedidos.

- Animación y seguimiento del forecast así como tareas que garantizan el nivel de servicio.




🔎 ¿Qué buscamos?:

Formación Superior en ADE, Ingenieria Industrial, Ingenieria Quimica, Grado en Matemáticas o similar.
Deseable haber realizado Erasmus o estancias en el extranjero por estudios o trabajo.
Experiencia de 1 año en puesto similar sobre todo en empresa de gran consumo y en análisis de datos.
Se valorará experiencia previa en departamentos de logística, demand.
Dominio avanzado de excel (tablas dinámicas, macros, formulación, ...)
Muy valorable conocimientos de herramientas de análisis estadístico: Matlab, SAS, Revolution Analytics, BI, ...
Capacidad de análisis, gestión y resolución.
Actitud positiva ante situaciones complejas.
Alto nivel de aprendizaje y compromiso con el trabajo.
Persona con entusiasmo, ganas de trabajar y actitud positiva.




✨ ¿Qué ofrecemos?

Horario: 40 horas semanale de Lunes a Viernes: L – J 9:00h a 18:30h y V 8:30h a 15:00h
Contrato Indefinido.
Ubicación del puesto: centro Barcelona.
Incorporación inmediata.

Title: Data Analyst (m/f/d)
Description:
InPost Group is an innovative European out of home deliveries company, revolutionizing the way parcels are delivered to customers. With operations across several countries, our network of intelligent lockers provides customers with a fast, convenient, and secure delivery option. InPost Group is a publicly traded company, with a market capitalization of about $5 billion as of March 2023. With over 10,000 people worldwide, InPost Group is one of the largest out of home delivery providers in Europe, committed to providing sustainable and efficient delivery solutions to meet the evolving needs of customers in today's rapidly changing landscape.





Job Description



In this role, you will be responsible for delivering high‑quality data, insights, and analytics that empower managers to make accurate, data‑driven product, campaign, and operational decisions. You will help optimize processes—such as merchant onboarding and servicing within the Pay product—by identifying trends, anomalies, and risks to support more effective (cost‑ and time‑efficient, and occasionally PR‑sensitive) management of the area.



Key Responsibilities



Power BI Reporting & Dashboard Management

Prepare, develop, and maintain Power BI reports (transactional and operational dashboards) to support effective decision‑making in product development, deployments, and marketing activities.



End‑to‑End Analytical Support

Conduct comprehensive analyses, interpret complex datasets, and present clear, concise conclusions and recommendations to internal and external stakeholders.



Trend & Anomaly Detection

Identify trends, patterns, deviations, and anomalies (e.g., campaign performance, transaction result outliers) and communicate findings to relevant teams for managerial action.



Data & Dashboard Optimization Activities

Participate in projects aimed at refining the maintenance, aggregation, and structuring of dashboards to improve efficiency and workspace organization within the Pay team.



Analytical Contribution to Business Cases

Support the development of business cases—including those requiring large or multi‑source datasets—for budgeting, financial planning, and strategic initiatives.



Business Analysis for Non‑Reported Areas

Perform business analyses for sales, product, growth, payments, or operational topics where no ongoing reporting exists or where data retrieval requires advanced database querying.



Coordination Between Data & Business Teams

Facilitate collaboration between Data and Business teams to streamline planning processes, enhance Data team understanding of business needs, and accelerate implementation timelines.





Qualifications


Proven ability to work with large datasets, including advanced SQL skills, query optimization, and reporting process automation (including VBA).
Experience with Business Intelligence tools, especially Power BI (Power Query, DAX).
Basic programming knowledge in Python (including PySpark).
Strong working knowledge of Databricks or similar platforms.
Highly developed analytical thinking and problem‑solving mindset.
Ability to translate data into actionable insights and business‑oriented recommendations.
Skill in creating clear, logical, user‑friendly, and visually appealing data visualizations and reports.
Solid understanding of statistics and mathematics at a practical level.


Additional Information



​​​​​​Why Join InPost?



The option to work from the office or 100% remotely
Opportunity to work in a diverse, international and cross-functional environment, along with leading experts.
Fulfilling careers with a range of benefits and invests in providing training opportunities for their development.
Involvement in technology monitoring and choices
Your impact will be visible instantly and you will be making a difference in our users lives
We offer B2B type of contract
Show more

Title: Junior Data Analyst (m/f/d)
Description:
ABOUT NUMA




Hey there! We’re Numa, a forward-thinking hospitality brand redefining how people discover cities. We create spaces where simplicity meets innovation, offering thoughtfully designed rooms, studios, and apartments in the heart of Europe’s most vibrant neighbourhoods.




We’re not just transforming the guest experience—we’re revolutionizing the hospitality industry from the inside out. Our cutting-edge technology streamlines operations, delivering unparalleled value with no hidden fees—just real, quality experiences.




At Numa, we believe that travel should be curious and authentic, and we bring those same values to our work culture. As part of our team, you’ll experience a dynamic and supportive environment where creativity thrives, and your contributions make a real impact. We’re committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace where every voice is valued, and different perspectives fuel our innovation.




ABOUT THE ROLE

● Extract and analyse data from multiple large-scale data warehouses, supporting the setup, maintenance, and integration of data sources.

● Develop dashboards and reporting to generate commercial insights across revenue functions, including Revenue Management, Marketing, Strategy, and other business teams.

● Proactively identify trends, patterns, and anomalies in data, and translate findings into actionable recommendations and process improvements.

● Collaborate with stakeholders to identify strategic opportunities and answer key business questions through data analysis.

● Present analyses and insights to a wide range of business stakeholders in a clear and compelling way.

● Enable stakeholders to independently leverage data by developing self-service dashboards and tools (e.g., Looker).

● Support the development, standardization, and rollout of internal reporting across the organization.

● Contribute to improving data quality, definitions, and governance within the Revenue Analytics domain.




ABOUT YOU

● Proficiency in SQL and experience with Looker (or similar BI tools such as Tableau, Power BI, etc.).

● Advanced knowledge of Google Sheets or Microsoft Excel.

● Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science), or Business Administration with a strong analytical focus.

● Interest in data analysis, visualization, statistics, and data storytelling.

● AI-first mindset with a strong curiosity for emerging AI technologies and applications.

● Strong problem-solving skills, hands-on mentality, and ability to think critically and work autonomously in iterative environments.

● Collaborative team player with strong communication skills.




ABOUT OUR OFFER

Empower your career: Thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment where your ideas help shape our future. Enjoy opportunities for professional growth and build a fulfilling career path.
Experience our brand: Immerse yourself in the Numa lifestyle with a complimentary stay. Share the love with friends and family through exclusive discounts on our stylish apartments. Connect with a global community of colleagues at our inspiring team-building events.




Numa values the diversity of the people it hires and serves. Diversity at our company means fostering a workplace in which individual differences are recognised, appreciated, respected and responded to in ways that fully develop and utilise each person’s talents and strengths. We therefore welcome applications from people of all races, ethnicities, disability statuses, ages, religions, gender identities, and sexual orientations.




For more information on the processing of your personal data, please see our Privacy Notice.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




CHI SIAMO:




SCS Consulting è una società di consulenza di direzione presente sul mercato da più di 40 anni che opera nel campo dei servizi professionali di consulenza, sviluppo delle competenze e valorizzazione delle risorse umane e che punta all’innovazione e al perseguimento della vision strategica dei propri clienti.

SCS Consulting opera su tutto il territorio nazionale, con un team costituito oggi da circa 200 professionisti, cui si affianca un consolidato network di partner per diversi settori specifici, nel supportare imprese e territori con servizi e soluzioni per la creazione di valore e la realizzazione di una crescita sostenibile, attraverso la visione integrata delle proprie aree di competenza: consulenza strategica e organizzativa, change management e sostenibilità.







IL PROFILO




In ottica di potenziamento della Divisione Finance, siamo alla ricerca di una/un Data Analyst con solida conoscenza di Excel e Power BI per supportare il nostro team di analisi dati. La risorsa lavorerà su progetti di business intelligence, reportistica e analisi dati, contribuendo allo sviluppo di dashboard e strumenti a supporto delle decisioni aziendali.




TASK PRINCIPALI:




• Analizzare e preparare dati provenienti da diverse fonti aziendali, garantendone qualità e coerenza

• Creare e aggiornare dashboard interattive in Power BI per il monitoraggio delle performance aziendali

• Utilizzare Power Query per la raccolta, trasformazione e integrazione dei dati da fonti multiple

• Sviluppare metriche e KPI in DAX per supportare analisi e decisioni operative

• Strutturare modelli dati funzionali alle analisi, garantendo affidabilità e semplicità di utilizzo

• Ottimizzare la user experience delle dashboard, rendendole intuitive e facilmente fruibili dagli stakeholder

• Pubblicare e gestire report tramite Power BI Service, assicurandone aggiornamento e condivisione

• Sviluppare e ottimizzare report su Excel, utilizzando formule avanzate, tabelle pivot e automazioni

• Collaborare con il team e le funzioni di business per identificare insight utili a supporto delle decisioni

• Documentare processi e logiche di calcolo per garantire la replicabilità delle analisi




Requisiti:




• 2–3 anni di esperienza in ambito data analysis/business intelligence.

• Ottima conoscenza di Excel, tabelle pivot, formule avanzate e Power Query.

• Buona conoscenza di Power BI (creazione di report, power query, modelli dati, DAX, Power BI Service).

• Capacità di trasformare dati in insight chiari e utilizzabili.

• Capacità di analisi e problem-solving, con attenzione ai dettagli.

• Propensione a lavorare in team e a interagire con stakeholder non tecnici.

• Interesse per il mondo della business intelligence e della data analytics.




Completano il profilo:




ottime doti di comunicazione con standing adeguato al ruolo
forte orientamento al team working, alla collaborazione e al supporto dei colleghi, mantenendo sempre un approccio positivo alle situazioni
essere costantemente focalizzato sul risultato, sulla responsabilità individuale e sulla qualità del lavoro
attenzione continua all’eccellenza tecnica ed al dettaglio




COSA OFFRIAMO




Le nostre persone sono il vero motore dell’impresa. SCS Consulting sviluppa la propria crescita attorno alla valorizzazione delle sue persone, investendo in modo continuo sulla loro crescita e percorso professionale, offrendo quindi un inquadramento e retribuzione in linea con esperienze e capacità del candidato, formazione continua e welfare aziendale.




Il candidato, quindi, avrà la concreta opportunità di una crescita professionale e di carriera all’interno di un ambiente dinamico e stimolante che promuove il confronto continuo e la proattività.




Avrà la possibilità di sviluppare un sistema di relazioni professionali attraverso l’interlocuzione con le prime linee di importanti aziende italiane ed internazionali, associazioni di riferimento di SCS, business school.




Inoltre, avrà la possibilità di lavorare in una realtà del mondo della consulenza strategica che basa da sempre il suo approccio di creazione di valore sulla contaminazione di esperienze e competenze di business, people management e sustainability.




COME CANDIDARSI




Inviare CV dettagliato accompagnato da mail di presentazione alla mail:

selezionifinance@scsconsulting.it

riportando nell’oggetto della mail: DataAnalyst_26




La modalità di lavoro prevista è ibrida: 60% di presenza (cliente e/o sede) e 40% di lavoro agile, su base settimanale, in coerenza con le effettive esigenze di progetto. È richiesta disponibilità a trasferte nazionali.




Sede di lavoro: Torino




In SCS crediamo fermamente in un ambiente rispettoso delle differenze, valorizziamo attivamente le unicità di ciascuno, secondo il merito e le competenze con particolare attenzione alla valorizzazione delle diversità di genere : SCS è certificata dal 2023 secondo gli standard stabiliti dalla prassi UNI/PdR 125:2022

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We’re excited to be hiring a Data Analyst to join a high-performing team supporting cross-functional client initiatives in Athens.




This is a fantastic opportunity for someone with strong analytical skills who enjoys turning data into meaningful business insights.




📍 Location: Athens, Greece (on-site)

⏳ Contract: 8-month contract starting September

💰 Salary: Up to €3,800 per month




🔍 The Role:

You’ll work closely with internal teams and clients to explore, analyse and visualise data, helping drive informed decision-making. The role sits within a collaborative, fast-paced environment where your insights will have real business impact.




🚀 Key Responsibilities:

Analyse large datasets to identify trends, insights and opportunities
Create compelling dashboards and data visualisations
Translate complex data into clear, actionable business insights
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver data-driven solutions
Support reporting, forecasting and segmentation activities
Apply statistical methods to enhance analysis and decision-making
Ensure data accuracy, quality and consistency




✅ What We’re Looking For:

3–5 years’ experience in data analytics or BI roles
Strong SQL skills and experience with data manipulation
Advanced Excel and PowerPoint skills
Experience with data visualisation tools and dashboards
Knowledge of statistical methods (SPSS/Modeler is a plus)
Strong communication skills with ability to simplify complex data
Fluency in Greek and English




🎯 Ideal Background:

Degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science or similar
Experience working with large, diverse datasets
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, client-facing environment

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
In 1966, in Marseille, Pierre Bellon laid the foundations of what would become Sodexo, guided by a simple yet powerful idea: improving everyday life through services that truly matter.

Today, Sodexo is a global leader in Food and Services, supporting people at every moment of life, at work, in schools, hospitals and communities. With 426,000 colleagues across 43 countries, we serve 80 million consumers every day, generating €24.1 billion in revenues. Our growth goes hand in hand with social and environmental responsibility, driven by our purpose: to create a better everyday for everyone and build a better life for all.




About Sodexo Business Services (SBS)




Portugal is no exception! Seven years ago, we established Sodexo Business Services (SBS), our shared service center. Here, you will have the opportunity to join various Financial teams such as Record to Report, Order to Cash, Purchase to Pay, Financial Analysis, and Master Data, supporting European markets and ensuring business growth.




Join Us!

We are looking for talented professionals to be part of our team, bringing unique skills and perspectives to help us grow even further.




Data Analyst

The Data Analyst is responsible for the technical execution of reporting and insight that enhances transparency & business performance with particular emphasis on digitization (Power BI), process efficiency & solution




⚙What will you do?

Enhancing our digital reporting suite & ensuring smooth execution of business-critical activities.
Demonstrate strong technical capability across reporting tools,
Accountable for scoping, designing, and delivering reporting developments.
Explore new ways to leverage reporting technologies, with a focus on building advanced proficiency in tools such as Power BI, Power Automate, and Excel.
Ensure that reporting output and architecture is coherent and consistent with our governance framework and standards.
Evaluate reporting processes and recommend improvements to enhance efficiency and reduce non‑value‑adding analysis.
Engage proactively with stakeholders to deliver reporting and insights, building effective business partnerships and communicating data in a clear, relatable narrative.
Translate broad or unclear stakeholder requirements into structured reporting plans and deliverables.
Be able to shift between data modelling, content creation, commercial insight & business partnering.




🤙What You Need to Succeed:

Bachelor’s degree in relevant field
Fluency in English required, ability to speak other European language is a plus
Focus on produt excellence, integrity and costumer focus
Experiency in Power BI, Power Automate, and Excel.
Continuous improvement mindset
Communication skills
Problem solving
Be flexible and versatile




What we have to offer you?

Hybrid working model;
Flexible working hours;
Health & Life Insurance;
Meal allowance paid in Meal Card;
Additional Days off: extra vacation day, employee’s birthday, volunteering day;
Opportunity to grow professionally inside the Company;
Possibility to participate in multicultural projects;
Several internal activities aiming to promote our team´s wellbeing.




📩 Apply now! Send your CV to Recruitment.SBS.Portugal@sodexo.com with the job title in the subject line. Explore all our career opportunities here: Sodexo Careers.

Learn more about Sodexo: YouTube - Sodexo.




Diversity & Inclusion Statement

Sodexo is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment based on merit.




🔒 Data Privacy:

We respect your privacy. Your personal data will be used exclusively for recruitment purposes, processed under GDPR regulations, and treated confidentially. For further details, contact privacy.sbs@sodexo.com.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Data Analyst




Job Type: 12-Month Contract




Job Description

Our client, a leading organisation, is seeking an experienced Data Analyst to join their team on an initial 12-month daily rate contract. This role will focus on analysing complex datasets, delivering business insights, and supporting strategic decision-making across key business functions.




Job Responsibilities

Gather, analyse and interpret large volumes of data from multiple business systems.
Produce meaningful reports, dashboards and visualisations to support operational and strategic decision-making.
Work closely with business stakeholders to understand reporting requirements and translate them into analytical solutions.
Identify trends, patterns and opportunities for process improvement through data analysis.
Develop and maintain reporting solutions using appropriate analytical and visualisation tools.
Ensure data quality, integrity and consistency across reporting environments.
Support ongoing data governance initiatives and contribute to continuous improvement of data management practices.
Collaborate with technical and business teams to deliver data-driven solutions within agreed timelines.




Experience Required

Minimum of 3+ years' experience in a Data Analyst or similar analytical role.
Strong experience analysing and interpreting complex datasets.
Proven ability to create reports, dashboards and management information for business stakeholders.
Experience working with SQL and relational databases.
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.
Strong analytical, problem-solving and critical thinking abilities.
Experience working within Agile or project-based delivery environments.
Ability to manage multiple priorities while maintaining a high level of accuracy.




Desirable Skills

Experience with Power BI, Tableau or similar visualisation tools.
Knowledge of Python or R for data analysis and automation.
Experience with cloud-based data platforms such as Azure, AWS or Google Cloud.
Understanding of data warehousing and ETL processes.
Familiarity with data governance, data quality and regulatory compliance principles.
Exposure to financial services, professional services or other highly regulated industries would be advantageous.




Educational Requirements

Bachelor's degree or equivalent third-level qualification in Computer Science, Data Analytics, Mathematics, Statistics, Information Systems or a related discipline.




HOW TO APPLY

If you are interested in this role, please apply with your updated CV.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
About us – Wewo Telecommunication Italy S.r.l.




Wewo Telecommunication Italy S.r.l. is part of the international Vivo group, a global leader in the production of smartphones and smart devices.

In Italy, we collaborate with major retail and distribution partners, delivering innovative technologies within a dynamic and international environment.




The Role




We are looking for a motivated and analytical Junior Data Analyst to support business performance monitoring and data-driven decision-making across the organization.

The role will be responsible for collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data from multiple sources to provide actionable insights and support business stakeholders. The Junior Data Analyst will work closely with Retail, Sales, Marketing, GTM, and Finance teams to monitor key performance indicators, sales trends, stock levels, promotional activities, and overall business performance.

This position has a direct impact on reporting accuracy, business planning, operational efficiency, and the identification of opportunities to improve retail performance and commercial results in Italy.




Main Responsibilities




Collect, clean, and analyze data from multiple sources.
Prepare recurring and ad-hoc reports for business stakeholders.
Monitor retail business performance indicators and support KPI tracking.
Analyze sell-in and sell-out performance across retail channels.
Track sales performance by product, model, retailer, region, and promoter.
Monitor stock levels, inventory aging, product availability, and replenishment trends.
Support retail visibility and merchandising performance analysis.
Develop dashboards and data visualizations.
Identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities through data analysis.
Support forecasting and business planning activities.
Collaborate with Retail, Sales, Marketing, GTM, and Finance teams.
Monitor promotional campaign performance and support ROI analysis.
Ensure data accuracy and consistency across reports and systems.
Assist in process automation and reporting optimization initiatives.




Requirements




Bachelor's Degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Engineering, Computer Science, Business Analytics, or related fields.
1-2 years of experience in Data Analysis, Business Analysis, Reporting, or similar roles.
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Excel (Pivot Tables, Power Query, advanced formulas).
Good knowledge of Power BI, Tableau, or similar Business Intelligence tools.
Basic knowledge of SQL.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Attention to detail and data accuracy.
Good communication and presentation skills.
Fluent Italian and good English communication skills.




Preferred Qualifications




Experience with Power BI dashboard development.
Knowledge of Python or R for data analysis.
Experience working with large datasets.
Understanding of retail, sales, and commercial KPIs.
Experience in consumer electronics, retail, FMCG, or telecommunications industries.
Knowledge of sell-in, sell-out, stock management, and retail performance metrics.




What We Offer




International and dynamic working environment.
Opportunity to work closely with Retail, Sales, GTM, Marketing, and Finance teams.
Professional growth and development opportunities.
Training and mentoring support.
Exposure to business-critical projects and decision-making processes.
Fixed-term contract (12 months) with possibility of extension based on business needs.
On-site working environment.




Salary Range




RAL: €25,000 - €28,000 based on experience and skills.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Would you like to join an international atmosphere and be involved in projects across Europe and with impact worldwide having the opportunity to consolidate your career? This is your opportunity!




🚀 We are looking for a Data Analyst to join our team!




The Data Analyst will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining data models and Power BI dashboards based on Apptio Cost & Financial Data Lake (CFDL) to support Aftersales performance, cost transparency, and financial steering. The role acts as a bridge between Aftersales business stakeholders, Finance, and IT/Data teams, ensuring high‑quality, actionable insights.




🔑 Key Responsibilities




- Develop and maintain Power BI reports and dashboards using data sourced from Apptio CFDL.

- Build and optimize data models (facts, dimensions, measures) aligned with Aftersales KPIs.

- Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and reconciliation with financial and operational sources.

- Perform ad‑hoc analyses to support decision‑making in Aftersales and Finance. Aftersales & Financial Steering

- Translate Aftersales business needs into analytical requirements and KPIs.

- Support cost transparency, profitability, and performance tracking across Aftersales activities.

- Collaborate with Finance to align reporting with financial controlling and cost allocation logic.

- Identify trends, risks, and improvement opportunities through data insights. Apptio / CFDL Ownership

- Act as subject matter expert for Apptio CFDL data structures and logic.

- Coordinate with IT / Data Platform teams on data ingestion, transformations, and enhancements. - Ensure correct usage of Apptio data for reporting and analysis purposes. Stakeholder Management

- Work closely with Aftersales stakeholders to understand reporting needs and priorities.

- Present insights in a clear, business‑oriented way to non‑technical audiences.

- Support users with Power BI adoption, interpretation of reports, and best practices.




🛠 Technical Skills




- Strong experience with Power BI (data modeling, DAX, performance optimization).

- Solid understanding of Apptio / TBM concepts, ideally with exposure to CFDL.

- Experience working with financial and cost data.

- Good knowledge of SQL and data analysis concepts.

- Understanding of data quality, controls, and reconciliation processes.




🌍 Business & Soft Skills




- Strong analytical and problem‑solving mindset.

- Ability to translate complex data into clear business insights.

- Experience working with cross‑functional teams (Aftersales, Finance, IT).

- Structured, detail‑oriented, and proactive way of working.

- Strong communication skills in English (Spanish/German is a plus). Nice to Have

- Experience in Aftersales, Automotive, or Shared Services environments.

- Knowledge of SAP (FI/CO, Aftersales‑related data).

- Experience with cost allocation models and profitability analysis.

- Previous work in international or multi‑country setups. Key Interfaces

- Aftersales Business Teams - Finance / Controlling - IT / Data Platform / Apptio Teams

- Power BI & Reporting Communities Success Indicators

- High adoption and satisfaction of Aftersales Power BI reports.

- Reliable, reconciled, and trusted data from Apptio CFDL.

- Clear contribution to Aftersales cost transparency and performance steering.

- Reduced manual reporting and ad‑hoc data requests.




⭐ Within our benefits as employee, we provide:




60% Mobile work
30 working days of holidays
Annual Training Plan
