Title: IT Support Specialist (Junior)
Company: Sheraton Parco de' Medici Rome Hotel
Description:
Entra a far parte del nostro team!

Per una delle nostre strutture alberghiere Cluster siamo alla ricerca di n. 1 IT Junior appassionato di informatica e desideroso di intraprendere un percorso professionale in una realtà alberghiera internazionale

La posizione ha carattere d'urgenza.

Principali responsabilità


Supporto tecnico agli utenti interni.
Installazione, configurazione e manutenzione di hardware e software.
Assistenza nella gestione della rete aziendale e dei dispositivi informatici.
Supporto nella risoluzione di problematiche tecniche.
Monitoraggio del corretto funzionamento dei sistemi IT.
Collaborazione con il team per l'implementazione di nuovi progetti tecnologici.


Requisiti


Diploma o Laurea ad indirizzo Informatico o affine.
Buona conoscenza dei sistemi operativi Windows e del pacchetto Microsoft Office.
Conoscenze di base di networking, hardware e software.
Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese.
Ottime capacità di problem solving.
Attitudine al lavoro in team e orientamento al servizio.
Precisione, affidabilità e voglia di apprendere.
Essere automuniti


Si offre


Contratto secondo il CCNL Turismo;
Retribuzione lorda annuale compresa tra €20000 e €22000;
Mensa aziendale;
Percorsi di formazione e sviluppo professionale;
Inserimento in un contesto internazionale e dinamico.


Sede di lavoro: Roma Fiumicino

La ricerca è rivolta a candidati di entrambi i sessi (L. 903/77 e D.Lgs. 198/2006).

Title: Stage — Data Analyst Junior · Finance ·Private Equity
Description:
YIAB & CO Advisory  Stage — Data Analyst Junior · Finance ·Private Equity

Lieu : Paris Durée : 4 à 5 mois Rémunération : Selon convention + avantages




Des milliards d'euros d'actifs illiquides. Des flux financiers complexes. Des régulateurs qui exigent une traçabilité totale. Et au milieu : de la donnée — souvent mal structurée, rarement automatisée, toujours critique.




YIAB & CO Advisory cherche un(e) stagiaire data analyst pour intégrer une équipe structurée qui construit les pipelines, les modèles et les outils qui transforment cette donnée brute en information fiable — celle sur laquelle des investisseurs institutionnels prennent des décisions




Ce que vous allez faire :

Automatisation & data engineering Construire des pipelines de données financières : appels de fonds, distributions, waterfall, réconciliations des positions. SQL, Python, Power Query, VBA et Excel, si vous le maîtrisez.

Automatiser la recherche et le suivi de produits d'investissements. Documenter les procédures. Identifier les failles dans les flux de données.

Title: Virtual Executive Assistant (Remote)
Description:
Virtual Executive Assistant (Remote)

Work Mode: Remote

Our Company is seeking a Virtual Executive Assistant to provide high-level administrative and organizational support to senior professionals. This fully remote role is ideal for individuals who are highly organized, proactive, and capable of managing multiple responsibilities while maintaining discretion and professionalism.

In this position, you will act as a key support partner, helping to streamline daily operations, manage schedules, coordinate communications, and ensure priorities are handled efficiently. Your attention to detail, strong communication skills, and ability to anticipate needs will play a critical role in enabling leadership to focus on strategic initiatives.

 

Job Responsibilities:

 

1) Calendar and Communication Management

Manage executive calendars, including scheduling meetings, appointments, and reminders
Handle incoming emails and communications, prioritizing and responding when appropriate
Maintain clear and professional communication on behalf of executives
Coordinate internal and external correspondence



2) Task and Workflow Coordination

Track tasks, deadlines, and priorities to ensure timely completion
Organize and follow up on action items from meetings and communications
Assist in managing day-to-day administrative workflows
Ensure all activities are properly documented and tracked



3) Meeting and Travel Support

Coordinate meeting logistics including agendas, materials, and virtual setup
Take notes during meetings and distribute summaries or action items
Arrange travel plans, accommodations, and itineraries when required
Ensure all scheduling aligns with executive priorities



4) Documentation and Administrative Support

Prepare, format, and organize documents, reports, and presentations
Maintain organized digital files and records
Assist with data entry and information management tasks
Ensure documentation is accurate and easily accessible



 

Required Qualifications:

Strong verbal and written communication skills in English
Previous administrative, executive assistant, or coordination experience preferred
Excellent organizational and time management abilities
High level of discretion and professionalism when handling sensitive information
Ability to multitask and manage competing priorities
Comfortable using digital tools, calendars, and collaboration platforms
Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills
Self-motivated and able to work independently in a remote environment
Adaptable and responsive to changing priorities



 

Benefits Package:

Competitive compensation based on experience
Performance-based incentives and recognition
Flexible remote working environment
Opportunities for professional growth and advancement
Ongoing training and development support
Collaborative and supportive team culture



 

We are committed to creating a professional and supportive remote environment where you can grow your skills while contributing meaningfully to executive operations.

 

Keywords:

Executive assistant • Virtual assistant • Remote administration • Calendar management • Executive support • Task coordination • Meeting scheduling • Travel coordination • Document management • Communication support • Time management • Organizational skills • Remote work • Professional support • Business operations • Career growth

Title: AI & Data Science Internship (f/m/x)
Description:
Our goal is to have a solar system on every roof, a storage unit in every house, and an electric car in every garage. Enpal makes this possible with an integrated total solution for decentralized energy—from solar systems and battery storage to wall boxes, smart meters, and heat pumps. At the heart of it all is our AI-powered platform Enpal.One+, which intelligently connects thousands of systems and efficiently optimizes electricity procurement and feed-in on the energy market.

Are you ready for solutions that are more than just a promise and bring real quality of life to thousands of households every day? What you create at Enpal will deliver clean electricity tomorrow and bring about lasting change in how we use energy.

Your Tasks


You will work on AI use cases across the full end-to-end development lifecycle, including:
Scoping and validating new AI use cases
Data engineering and data analysis
Building prototypes and optimizing models and prompts
Evaluating machine learning models and large language models (LLMs)
Supporting rollout, deployment, and monitoring of production AI systems

Your Profile


You are currently studying Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Physics, Engineering, or a related field
You have gained first hands-on experience through internships, working student roles, or personal projects in AI, Machine Learning, or Data Science
You have experience in at least one of the following areas: Large Language Models (LLMs), Machine Learning, Deep Learning, or Statistics
You have strong programming skills in Python and SQL. Experience with other tools and technologies (e.g., APIs, cloud platforms, databases, or MLOps tools) is a plus
You are fluent in English. Fluency in German is a strong plus.


Your Benefits


Work at Germany's first green unicorn - Play an active role in shaping the solar energy transition.
The sun shines all over the world - At Enpal, you'll work with a highly motivated, diverse team of over 65 nationalities.
The ideal setup for your focus - We are convinced that excellent results are achieved when the conditions are right. That's why we rely on a hybrid working concept that gives you the freedom to always work in the way that best suits your tasks. Do you need new inspiration on an international level? With Workflex, we offer you the opportunity to relocate abroad for up to 30 days a year, depending on your responsibilities.
Grown-up business & startup spirit - In our modern office in Berlin-Friedrichshain, you'll find everything your heart desires, from height-adjustable desks and table tennis to stocked beverage fridges and barista coffee.
Your kick-start at Enpal - Onboarding day with a welcome bag, buddy program, and a team that really supports you.
Stay up to date & help shape the future - Transparent all-hands meetings, short decision-making processes, and an open feedback culture - without mistakes, there is no progress.
The energy transition can only be achieved together - At Enpal, you can expect legendary team spirit and unforgettable team events.
Your extra boost - 29 vacation days + one additional day off on Christmas Eve and New Year's Eve, discounted membership at Wellhub, and corporate benefits. Everything you need to help you achieve a good work-life balance.


At Enpal, we take pride in the diversity of our team. We do not make hiring or employment decisions based on race, religion or religious beliefs, ethnic or national origin, nationality, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, or age. Enpal is committed to fostering a safe and inclusive workplace and maintains a zero-tolerance policy toward discrimination and harassment of any kind.

Title: Remote Computer User Support
Description:
Join our growing team as a Remote Computer User Support Specialist who will provide technical assistance and user support across our digital platforms. This role is ideal for individuals who enjoy troubleshooting, helping users resolve issues, and working in a structured remote environment.

In this position, you will assist users with technical concerns, guide them through solutions, and ensure systems are functioning effectively. Your ability to communicate clearly and solve problems efficiently will support smooth day-to-day operations.

 

What You’ll Be Doing:

Respond to user support requests via email, chat, or ticketing systems
Troubleshoot basic hardware, software, and system-related issues
Guide users through step-by-step solutions in a clear and professional manner
Assist with account setup, access issues, and password resets
Document support cases, resolutions, and system updates
Escalate complex issues to higher-level technical teams when required
Maintain and update support documentation or knowledge bases
Identify recurring issues and report trends for improvement



 

What We’re Looking For:

Basic understanding of computer systems and troubleshooting
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills
Clear written and verbal communication abilities
Ability to explain technical concepts in simple terms
Comfortable using support tools or ticketing systems (training provided if needed)
Ability to manage multiple requests and prioritize tasks
Self-motivated and able to work independently
Previous IT or support experience is a plus but not required



 

Tools & Work Environment:

Remote support and troubleshooting tools
Email, chat, and video communication platforms
Internal documentation and knowledge base systems



 

Benefits:

Competitive compensation
Flexible remote working schedule
Training and onboarding support
Opportunities for career growth in IT support
Performance-based incentives
Supportive and collaborative remote team
Access to learning and development resources



 

Why Join:

This role offers hands-on experience in technical support and remote user assistance. You will develop practical skills in troubleshooting, communication, and system support, creating a strong foundation for growth in IT and technical operations.

Title: Data Management Officer (DMO) - H/F
Description:
Description Du Poste

Data Management Officer (DMO) SQL // H/F

🎯 Votre rôle

Vous rejoignez le Crédit Agricole d’Île-de-France dans un contexte clé : la création du Data Management Office (DMO).

Ici, tout est à construire.

L’objectif est clair : unifier, structurer et fiabiliser la donnée pour en faire un socle commun utilisé par l’ensemble des métiers de la Caisse Régionale.

Votre rôle est central. Vous n’êtes pas là que pour produire de la donnée au sens technique, mais pour définir la manière dont elle doit exister, être organisée et utilisée.

Vous intervenez au croisement de la gouvernance, des métiers et de la data, dans un environnement bancaire exigeant, notamment au regard des enjeux réglementaires (BCBS 239).

Vous contribuez directement à faire de la donnée un actif stratégique, fiable et piloté.

🧩 Vos missions

Votre quotidien s’articule autour de plusieurs dimensions complémentaires :

🔹 Gouvernance de la donnée

Vous définissez et structurez les règles de gestion de la donnée. Vous posez un cadre clair sur les méthodes de calcul, les référentiels et les usages afin de garantir une cohérence globale à l’échelle de l’organisation.

🔹 Qualité & fiabilité des données

Vous analysez les données existantes, identifiez les écarts et mettez en place des règles de contrôle. Vous pilotez des indicateurs de qualité et inscrivez la donnée dans une logique d’amélioration continue.

🔹 Structuration & modélisation

À partir de données brutes, vous êtes capable de :


recréer des tables pertinentes
redéfinir des logiques de calcul
organiser et ordonnancer les flux
construire des référentiels métiers fiables


Vous travaillez dans des environnements SQL, avec une logique de structuration et non de développement pur.

La compétence en développement SQL est impérative pour votre réussite en poste

🔹 Pilotage de projets data

Vous intervenez comme un véritable chef de projet sur les sujets data :


compréhension des besoins métiers
cadrage des usages
priorisation des sujets
coordination avec les équipes techniques


Vous assurez la bonne mise en œuvre des évolutions dans une logique de time to market.

🔹 Interaction métiers & accompagnement

Vous êtes l’interlocuteur privilégié des métiers sur les sujets data. Vous les accompagnez dans la compréhension, l’utilisation et l’appropriation des données mises à disposition.

🔹 Conformité & réglementation

Vous intégrez les contraintes réglementaires dans vos travaux, notamment :


BCBS 239
exigences de traçabilité
qualité et auditabilité des données


Profil recherché

🧠 Votre profil

Vous disposez d’une formation supérieure en data, informatique ou équivalent, et d’une expérience significative dans des environnements data structurants.

Vous maîtrisez les enjeux de gouvernance de la donnée, qualité et référentiels.

Vous êtes un développeur SQL senior et vous souhaitez donner un coup d’accélérateur à votre carrière.

Vous comprenez les enjeux réglementaires liés à la data, notamment en environnement bancaire.

Vous savez faire le lien entre des enjeux métiers concrets et des problématiques de structuration de la donnée.

🧬 Votre posture

Ce poste s’adresse à une personne qui aime structurer, clarifier et construire.

Vous êtes rigoureux, capable de poser un cadre là où il n’existe pas encore, tout en avançant dans un environnement en construction.

Vous avez une vraie capacité à prendre du recul, à analyser des situations complexes et à proposer des solutions concrètes.

Vous êtes à l’aise dans la collaboration transverse et savez embarquer des interlocuteurs variés.

Vous aimez les environnements exigeants, où l’impact est réel et visible.

🌱 Pourquoi rejoindre ce projet

Vous participez à l'expansion d’un Data Management Office, avec un rôle structurant et visible.

Vous intervenez sur des sujets à fort enjeu stratégique pour la banque.

Vous contribuez directement à transformer la manière dont la donnée est utilisée, pilotée et valorisée.

C’est un terrain de jeu rare pour quelqu’un qui veut construire, structurer et avoir un impact durable.

Title: Customer Master Data Specialist
Description:
Job Purpose




The main purpose of this role is to perform Customer Master Data maintenance in SAP S/4HANA and support controlled master data processes through MDG / approved workflow channels. The role acts as the main point of contact (SPOC) for all types of requests concerning the creation, extension, update, blocking, and maintenance of Customer Master Data, ensuring the quality, integrity, compliance, and governance of Master Data.




Principal Responsibilities

Responsible for timely and accurate processing of internal requests to create new customer accounts or make necessary updates to already existing Customer Master Data in SAP S/4HANA.
Process requests within given SLA’s and provide updates to requestors in a timely manner.
Validate requests and provide requestors with feedback regarding the correctness and completeness of data input prior to creation or change.
Cooperate with relevant departments to obtain the necessary approvals prior to data entry or data changes in SAP.
Ensure the integrity, relevance, compliance, and cleanliness of Customer Master Data in key systems and support the application of group Master Data governance standards.
Maintain Customer Master Data across the relevant Business Partner structures and organizational views required to support operational and financial processes.
Perform regular checks and support data cleansing activities to remove duplicate, obsolete, incomplete, or unused data and improve data quality.
Archive all relevant approvals and supporting documentation for audit purposes.
Execute relevant data quality controls and contribute to KPI reporting, service level improvements, and ongoing process performance monitoring.
Support the maintenance of procedures, policies, work instructions, and training material related to Customer Master Data processes.
Collaborate with Internal Control, Finance, Accounts Receivable, Sales, Trade Compliance, and other relevant stakeholders to ensure complete and compliant setup of customer data.
Ensure all working practices follow Guerbet standard policies and procedures, including health & safety, internal control, and equal opportunities.




Experience / Skills:

Experience with data administration would be an asset.
Experience with SAP S/4HANA and/or Master Data Governance processes would be an asset.
Proficient in English.
Good personal computer skills.
Driven by deadlines.
Proactive and able to demonstrate use of initiative.
Adaptable with an ability to change directions and priorities if required.
Ability to work independently.
Conscientious and with an eye for detail.
Analytical skills.
Ability to understand the business impact of Customer Master Data on downstream processes.

Title: Ingeniero de Procesos Junior
Description:
Descripción del puesto En el puesto de Ingeniero de Procesos Junior en MÁS HUMANOS participarás en el análisis, diseño y mejora de procesos productivos y de soporte dentro de la empresa. En tu día a día colaborarás con el equipo técnico para recoger datos, documentar procesos, elaborar diagramas de flujo y proponer mejoras orientadas a la eficiencia, la seguridad y la calidad. Apoyarás en la implementación y seguimiento de indicadores de control de procesos, así como en la identificación de desviaciones y la propuesta de acciones correctivas. También trabajarás con herramientas de análisis de datos y software específico de ingeniería de procesos para apoyar la toma de decisiones. Este es un puesto a jornada completa, presencial y ubicado en Zaragoza.

Requisitos

Aptitudes en diseño de procesos y control de procesos, con capacidad para documentar procedimientos y establecer parámetros de operación.
Conocimientos básicos de ingeniería de procesos y nociones de ingeniería química para comprender operaciones unitarias y flujos de producción.
Aptitudes analíticas sólidas, capacidad para interpretar datos, realizar cálculos técnicos y detectar oportunidades de mejora.
Titulación universitaria en Ingeniería de Procesos, Ingeniería Química, Ingeniería Industrial o estudios afines.
Manejo de herramientas ofimáticas (especialmente hojas de cálculo), y valorable experiencia con software de simulación o modelado de procesos.
Capacidad para trabajar en equipo, actitud proactiva, organización en el trabajo y ganas de aprender y desarrollarte en un entorno técnico.

Title: Supply Chain Analyst
Company: APCCI (Association des Professionnels de Conformité de Côte d’Ivoire)
Description:
Job Title: Supply Chain Analyst




Location: France




Job Type: Full-Time




Job Overview

We are seeking a highly analytical and detail-oriented Supply Chain Analyst (H/F) to support and optimize supply chain operations. The successful candidate will analyze supply chain performance, identify improvement opportunities, and provide data-driven recommendations to enhance efficiency, reduce costs, and improve overall business performance.

Key Responsibilities
Analyze supply chain data, including inventory, procurement, transportation, and distribution metrics.
Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and prepare regular reports for management.
Identify trends, inefficiencies, and opportunities for process optimization.
Collaborate with procurement, logistics, operations, and planning teams to improve supply chain performance.
Support demand forecasting, inventory planning, and supply chain strategy initiatives.
Evaluate supplier and transportation performance.
Develop dashboards and analytical reports using business intelligence tools.
Assist in implementing supply chain improvement projects and process enhancements.
Ensure data accuracy and maintain supply chain databases and reporting systems.
Requirements
Bachelor's degree in Supply Chain Management, Logistics, Business Administration, Economics, Engineering, or a related field.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel.
Excellent communication and organizational abilities.
Ability to interpret large datasets and present actionable insights.
Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment.
Preferred Qualifications
Previous experience in supply chain analysis, logistics, procurement, or operations.
Experience with ERP systems such as SAP, Oracle, or Microsoft Dynamics.
Knowledge of Power BI, Tableau, SQL, or other data visualization and reporting tools.
Familiarity with demand planning, inventory management, and forecasting techniques.
APICS/CSCP, CPIM, or related certifications are a plus.

Title: Consultant BI (Talend / PowerBI) H/F
Description:
Synanto (anciennement 2S2I) est une ESN à taille humaine avec un management de proximité, une culture et des valeurs qui inscrivent nos collaborateurs au cœur de son développement. En effet, la relation avec nos consultants est un des leviers de notre proposition de valeur nous permettant d'instaurer une relation durable, tout en les accompagnant à devenir des experts.

En ce sens, intégrer Synanto - devenir un Talent ∑ - c'est l'assurance de se voir proposer des missions stimulantes et d'évoluer en ayant un véritable plan de carrière.

Nos secteurs d'intervention sont variés (Industrie, Energie, Transports, Distribution, Edition de logiciels ou Secteur public), avec une expertise qui reste dominante dans le secteur Banque, Finance et Assurance.

Aujourd'hui, Synanto, Ce Sont


4 agences en France (Paris, Lyon, Aix-en-Provence et Montpellier) et 2 à l'étranger (Luxembourg et Tunisie)
150 collaborateurs
1 spécialisation dans le domaine Banque | Finance | Assurance
1 promesse : Vos Projets, Nos Talents, Une Rencontre
5 valeurs fortes : Synergie, Entraide, Transparence, Diversité, Optimisme


De nombreux talents ont déjà rejoint notre équipe, pour une vision différente, des opportunités de carrière et des projets innovants. Pourquoi pas vous !?... Venez écrire l'histoire d'une entreprise, experte des SI à dimension humaine.

POSTE / MISSIONS

En tant que Consultant BI, vous intervenez auprès de nos clients sur des projets à forte valeur ajoutée autour de la data.

À Ce Titre, Vous Serez Amené(e) à


Recueillir et analyser les besoins métiers
Concevoir et modéliser des entrepôts de données (datawarehouse / datamart)
Développer et maintenir des flux de données avec Talend (ETL)
Créer des tableaux de bord et reportings avec Power BI
Assurer la qualité, la fiabilité et la performance des données
Accompagner les utilisateurs dans la prise en main des outils (formation, support)
Participer aux phases de recette et de mise en production


PROFIL RECHERCHÉ

Compétences Techniques


Maîtrise de Talend (ETL)
Bonne maîtrise de Power BI (modélisation, DAX, data visualisation)
Solides connaissances en SQL et bases de données (PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server…)
Bonne compréhension des concepts BI : modélisation, ETL, datawarehouse


Compétences Complémentaires


Connaissances en data visualisation et data storytelling
Notions en gouvernance de la donnée appréciées


Vos qualités


Esprit analytique et sens du détail
Bon relationnel et capacité à interagir avec des interlocuteurs métiers
Autonomie, rigueur et proactivité
Esprit d'équipe


AVANTAGES

selon le profil

Title: Internship Data Analyst (all humans) - Studentjob.at
Description:
Make a difference in the financial life of millions of people: At Erste Digital you are co-creating the digital future, in which better financial health is possible. #believeinyourself

We are part of Erste Group - the largest banking group in Central and Eastern Europe with more than 2,500 branches and over 45,000 employees. Our more than 2,000 IT experts and enthusiasts are the bank's Digital Muscle.

We are the primary contact for any reporting and data analysis inquiries for the Holding, EBOe, and SavingsBanks. Our team creates tailored Adhoc queries and reports for customers from all departments of the bank.

What to do:



Create reports, analyses, evaluations, and data visualizations to support business decision-making.
Develop and maintain database queries using SQL.
Prepare, cleanse, and validate data to ensure high data quality.
Work independently on analytical tasks while collaborating closely with business stakeholders and specialist departments.
Assist in identifying trends, patterns, and insights from complex datasets.



You check these boxes:



Currently enrolled in a university or university of applied sciences (FH) program, preferably in Data Science, Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Business Analytics, or a related field.
Good understanding of relational databases and hands-on experience with SQL.
Experience with Python and/or R is an advantage.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
Fluent in German and English, both written and spoken.
A curious mindset with a passion for learning and continuous improvement.
Enthusiasm for teamwork and delivering high-quality results.



Why you will like us:



Possibility to benefit and learn from our international, talented and passionate community at Erste
Great learning curve through ownership and creative freedom
Interest groups and clubs: Take part in discussions and get involved in projects. Numerous interest groups invite you to engage with a topic
Good food: Healthy nutrition and good food are important for our quality of life. With seven restaurants and cafeterias, there's something to suit every taste on the Erste Campus
Health Centre - The Erste Campus operates a fully equipped health centre that offers all employees a high standard of medical services during working hours.
New way of work: With our flexible working hours model we want to make your life less complicated. Combing the best of both worlds, remote and company time.
Discover all our benefits of Erste group here - Thanks to our corporate benefits partners, you can also receive a whole bunch of discounts in different areas.
We offer you a varied part-time internship position (20-30h/week) for 12 months. An internship salary of 3267,00€ for Bachelor Students or 3400,00€ for Master Students (gross per month on a full-time basis) is offered.



The way we are:

Erste Group considers the diversity of its employees as key to innovation and success. As employer we are proud to offer everyone equal chances, irrespective of age, skin colour, religious belief, gender, sexual orientation or origin.

Title: Head of Data & AI
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Head of Data & AI based in France.

As the Head of Data & AI, you will lead the strategic vision and execution of a unified data organization spanning Data Engineering, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Quantitative Analytics, and Business Intelligence. In this leadership role, you will shape scalable data platforms, drive AI innovation, and build high-performing teams that transform complex data into measurable business value. Working at the intersection of engineering, analytics, and emerging AI technologies, you'll define technical roadmaps, establish best practices, and collaborate with senior stakeholders to support business growth. This is an outstanding opportunity for an experienced data leader to influence both technology strategy and organizational direction in a fast-paced, innovation-driven environment.

Accountabilities


Define and execute the overall Data & AI strategy, aligning data engineering, analytics, quantitative research, and AI initiatives with long-term business objectives.
Lead and mentor multidisciplinary teams while establishing technical standards, career development frameworks, and a culture of collaboration and continuous improvement.
Design and evolve scalable, reliable, and secure data platform architectures, overseeing ETL/ELT pipelines, real-time data processing, and cloud infrastructure.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
DATA ANALYST

Your data expertise can directly steer the brand strategy of a global retail giant while you spearhead a high-impact data migration from legacy systems to SQL. If you are looking for a highly visible data role where your interactive dashboards directly influence executive decisions, this long-term opportunity offers the perfect blend of technical ownership and strategic business impact.




ROLE OVERVIEW

Joining the Product Development Licensing and Branding team for a Fortune 100 retail leader based in Framingham, MA, you will act as a hybrid data strategist and analyst. In this long-term contract role with strong conversion potential, you will lead the transformation of legacy Access environments into advanced SQL data marts and dynamic Power BI dashboards. Working a predictable hybrid schedule (in-office Tuesday and Wednesday, with fully remote flexibility during quarter-end), you will turn massive, complex datasets into the actionable insights that executives rely on to manage critical brand partnerships.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Lead the technical transition of legacy Microsoft Access databases into a high-performance, SQL-based data mart to ensure data integrity.
Design, develop, and maintain interactive Power BI dashboards that visualize key business metrics for cross-functional stakeholders.
Analyze massive, complex datasets—including purchase orders and contract terms—using advanced SQL queries to identify commercial trends and process improvements.
Partner closely with multi-functional internal teams to gather data requirements and deliver tailored business intelligence solutions.
Present data-driven reports and performance summaries to leadership and brand partners, directly influencing brand management strategies.
Document new data processes and deliver light training to ensure the broader team is empowered to leverage your Power BI solutions.




WHAT YOU BRING

5+ years of experience in a data analysis, business intelligence, or highly analytical corporate operations role.
Expert-level proficiency in SQL, including database development, writing advanced queries, and data transformation processes.
Proven hands-on experience building interactive dashboards from scratch in Power BI, alongside strong legacy skills in MS Access and Excel.
Excellent communication and presentation skills, with a proven ability to translate complex data findings into compelling business narratives for leadership.
A highly organized, accountable working style with a natural knack for driving process automation and efficiency.
Legal authorization to work in the United States without current or future sponsorship requirements.




WHY THIS ROLE

High Visibility & Real Impact: You are not just pulling data; you are building the foundational analytical engine used to guide brand performance and contract adherence for a massive global corporation.
Resume-Building Tech Migration: Leading an Access-to-SQL migration provides high-value, modern architecture experience that proves your ability to transform corporate data environments.
The Best of Both Worlds: Enjoy a highly collaborative hybrid schedule with predictable in-office days, balanced by 100% remote weeks during high-intensity quarter-close periods.
True Contract-to-Career Pathway: This position is funded for the long haul by a business unit actively looking for long-term alignment and future conversion opportunities.




COMPENSATION

This engagement offers a competitive hourly rate tailored to your level of database development and business intelligence expertise.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Description

We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Junior Data Analyst to join our dynamic team. This entry-level position offers an excellent opportunity for individuals who are passionate about data and eager to develop their analytical skills in a professional environment. As a Junior Data Analyst, you will collaborate with various departments to collect, process, and analyze data to support business decision-making. You will work under the guidance of senior analysts and managers to transform raw data into meaningful insights that drive strategic initiatives. The ideal candidate should have a strong foundation in data analysis techniques, be proficient with data tools, and possess a keen eye for accuracy and problem-solving. In this role, you will gain hands-on experience with data visualization, reporting, and database management, contributing directly to the company’s growth and operational efficiency. This position is perfect for someone looking to kick-start their career in data analytics and grow their expertise in a supportive and innovative workplace. Join us and be part of a team that values curiosity, collaboration, and continuous learning, all while making a tangible impact through data-driven insights.

Responsibilities


Collect, clean, and validate data from various sources to ensure accuracy and completeness.
Assist in the development and maintenance of dashboards and reports for internal stakeholders.
Conduct basic statistical analysis to identify trends, patterns, and insights.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand data requirements and provide analytical support.
Prepare visualizations and presentations to communicate findings effectively to non-technical audiences.
Support senior analysts in complex data projects and ad hoc reporting tasks.
Monitor data quality and identify areas for process improvement or data enhancement.


Requirements


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, or a related field.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or Power BI.
Basic knowledge of SQL for querying databases.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to work collaboratively in a team-oriented environment.
Eagerness to learn new technologies and data analysis methodologies.

Description:
We are looking to hire a data analyst to join our data team. You will take responsibility for managing our master data set, developing reports, and troubleshooting data issues. To do well in this role you need a very fine eye for detail, experience as a data analyst, and a deep understanding of the popular data analysis tools and databases. Data Analyst Responsibilities: Managing master data, including creation, updates, and deletion. Managing users and user roles. Provide quality assurance of imported data, working with quality assurance analysts if necessary. Commissioning and decommissioning of data sets. Processing confidential data and information according to guidelines. Helping develop reports and analyses. Managing and designing the reporting environment, including data sources, security, and metadata. Supporting the data warehouse in identifying and revising reporting requirements. Supporting initiatives for data integrity and normalization. Assessing tests and implementing new or upgraded software and assisting with strategic decisions on new systems. Generating reports from single or multiple systems. Troubleshooting the reporting database environment and reports. Evaluating changes and updates to source production systems. Training end-users on new reports and dashboards. Providing technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing. Data Analyst Requirements: Bachelor s degree from an accredited university or college in computer science. Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field. Ability to work with stakeholders to assess potential risks. Ability to analyze existing tools and databases and provide software solution recommendations. Ability to translate business requirements into nontechnical, lay terms. High-level experience in methodologies and processes for managing large-scale databases. Demonstrated experience in handling large data sets and relational databases. Understanding of addressing and metadata standards. High-level written and verbal communication skills.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Data Analyst

About Busek

Busek Co. Inc. is a pioneer in advanced space propulsion and technology. Located in Natick, MA, we develop innovative solutions that enable the future of space exploration. Our products and technologies include electric propulsion systems, chemical propulsion, and advanced material development. Join our team to work on cutting-edge projects that shape the space industry.

Position Overview

Busek is seeking a Data Analyst to support the analysis of test and production data for advanced space propulsion systems. This role will work closely with Engineering, Test Operations, and Manufacturing to transform large volumes of technical data into meaningful insights that improve product performance, manufacturing processes, and production readiness.

The ideal candidate is analytical, curious, and self-motivated, with the ability to work independently and quickly learn new systems and technologies. While experience with electric propulsion is beneficial, candidates with strong data analysis skills and experience supporting engineering or manufacturing organizations are encouraged to apply.

Key Responsibilities


Analyze data generated from engineering testing, production, and manufacturing activities to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities for improvement
Develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations that communicate technical findings to engineering and leadership teams
Partner with Engineering, Manufacturing, and Test Operations to support product development and production initiatives
Validate data quality and ensure the integrity of engineering and manufacturing datasets
Support root cause investigations through data analysis and statistical evaluation
Assist in developing automated reporting and data analysis tools to improve efficiency
Document analysis methods, assumptions, and results to support engineering decisions
Present findings and recommendations clearly to technical and non-technical audiences
Support continuous improvement initiatives by identifying opportunities to improve data collection, reporting, and decision-making


Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Data Analytics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Physics, Statistics, or a related technical field
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex technical data
Experience analyzing large datasets using Excel and other data analysis tools
Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and learn new technologies with minimal supervision
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Attention to detail and a commitment to producing accurate, actionable analysis


Preferred Skills


Experience using Python, MATLAB, SQL, Power BI, Tableau, or similar analytical tools
Experience supporting engineering, manufacturing, aerospace, or other technical organizations
Familiarity with statistical analysis and data visualization techniques
Experience working with production, manufacturing, or test data
Knowledge of electric propulsion, aerospace systems, or other advanced engineering technologies is a plus but not required


Benefits


Health, dental, and vision insurance
Paid time off and holidays
Retirement savings plan (401k)
Professional development opportunities
Collaborative and innovative work environment


What We Offer


Opportunity to work on cutting-edge space propulsion technology
Exposure to advanced engineering, testing, and manufacturing operations
Opportunity to influence product development and production decisions through meaningful data analysis
Collaborative environment working alongside engineers developing next-generation space systems


U.S. Citizenship Requirement

Due to the nature of work performed under U.S. government contracts and applicable security and contractual requirements, this position is limited to U.S. citizens.

Title: Content Data Analyst
Description:
Your work days are brighter here.

We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us. As a Fortune 500 company and a leading AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, we’re shaping the future of work so teams can reach their potential and focus on what matters most. The minute you join, you’ll feel it. Not just in the products we build, but in how we show up for each other. Our culture is rooted in integrity, empathy, and shared enthusiasm. We’re in this together, tackling big challenges with bold ideas and genuine care. We look for curious minds and courageous collaborators who bring sun-drenched optimism and drive. Whether you're building smarter solutions, supporting customers, or creating a space where everyone belongs, you’ll do meaningful work with Workmates who’ve got your back. In return, we’ll give you the trust to take risks, the tools to grow, the skills to develop and the support of a company invested in you for the long haul. So, if you want to inspire a brighter work day for everyone, including yourself, you’ve found a match in Workday, and we hope to be a match for you too.

About The Team

It's fun to work in a company where people truly believe in what they're doing. At Workday, we're committed to bringing passion for data and a focus on actionable insights to the Marketing organization. Our Content Marketing Analytics team plays a key role in helping the broader Marketing team understand what's working, where to invest, and how to continuously improve the content experiences we create for our audiences.

We help shape content strategy through data—turning complex performance metrics into clear, meaningful recommendations that guide how we plan, create, and distribute content. Whether it's identifying what resonates with our audiences, tracking performance against goals, or uncovering new opportunities through digital analytics, our work directly influences how Workday shows up in the market.

We work hard, and we're serious about what we do. But we like to have a good time, too. In fact, we run our company with that principle in mind every day: one of our core values is fun.

About The Role

As a Digital Content Data Analyst, you will be a key member of the Content Marketing Analytics team, responsible for owning and managing the end-to-end analytics process for content marketing. You will use tools like Adobe Analytics, Snowflake, Sigma, ContentSquare, BrandWatch, and Google Search Console to track, analyze, and report on digital content performance across channels. Your insights will help the team make informed decisions to optimize campaigns, improve customer experiences, and contribute to business growth.

In this role, you will be responsible for:


Determining strategic goals and direction for Content Marketing using data and analysis
Owning and managing the end-to-end analytics process for Content Marketing, including data collection, reporting, and insight generation across platforms such as Adobe Analytics, Sigma, ContentSquare, BrandWatch, and Google Search Console
Delivering quarterly analytics reviews that include blog performance, goal tracking, and strategic recommendations for upcoming quarters and annual planning
Representing Content Marketing in cross-functional discussions related to a Corporate Communications analytics framework
Collaborating with the broader Marketing Insights Systems Orchestration team on cross-functional projects and data initiatives
Transforming raw data into clear, compelling narratives that inform content strategy and help partners across Marketing understand performance trends


About You

Basic Qualifications


5+ years of related experience in data analytics, digital analytics, or a similar role
Experience with Adobe Analytics or a similar web analytics platform
Experience working with data warehousing tools such as Snowflake or similar platforms


Other Qualifications


Skilled in data analysis, with experience inspecting, cleansing, transforming, and modeling data to discover useful information and support decision-making, using tools and techniques such as statistical analysis, data visualization, and SQL
Experienced in analytical thinking, with the ability to break down complex data into fundamental parts, identify patterns and trends in datasets, and apply logical reasoning to solve problems and inform strategy
Proficient in data operations, with an understanding of the collection, processing, storage, and distribution of data, including experience improving data quality and implementing effective data governance practices
Knowledgeable in business analysis, with the ability to evaluate organizational operations, interpret data to identify areas for improvement, and provide recommendations that support strategic planning and decision-making
Experienced in data transformation, with the ability to convert data from one format or structure into another, clean and standardize data for analysis, and design effective transformation workflows
Capable of sound decision making, with the ability to evaluate multiple options, assess potential outcomes, and select the best course of action to support content marketing goals and operational improvements
Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to present complex data findings clearly to diverse audiences and collaborate effectively across teams


Workday Pay Transparency Statement

The annualized base salary ranges for the primary location and any additional locations are listed below. Workday pay ranges vary based on work location. As a part of the total compensation package, this role may be eligible for the Workday Bonus Plan or a role-specific commission/bonus, as well as annual refresh stock grants. Recruiters can share more detail during the hiring process. Each candidate’s compensation offer will be based on multiple factors including, but not limited to, geography, experience, skills, job duties, and business need, among other things. For more information regarding Workday’s comprehensive benefits, please click here.

Primary Location: USA.CA.Pleasanton

Primary Location Base Pay Range: $118,600 USD - $177,800 USD

Additional US Location(s) Base Pay Range: $100,100 USD - $177,800 USD

Our Approach to Flexible Work

With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in-office each week, we simply spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.

Pursuant to applicable Fair Chance law, Workday will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

Workday is an Equal Opportunity Employer including individuals with disabilities and protected veterans.

At Workday, we are committed to providing an accessible and inclusive hiring experience where all candidates can fully demonstrate their skills. If you require assistance or an accommodation at any point, please email accommodations@workday.com.

Are you being referred to one of our roles? If so, ask your connection at Workday about our Employee Referral process!

At Workday, we value our candidates’ privacy and data security. Workday will never ask candidates to apply to jobs through websites that are not Workday Careers.

Please be aware of sites that may ask for you to input your data in connection with a job posting that appears to be from Workday but is not.

In addition, Workday will never ask candidates to pay a recruiting fee, or pay for consulting or coaching services, in order to apply for a job at Workday.

Title: Data Analyst Internship
Description:
About ConnectPrep: Our goal is to redefine "tutoring" - to use data, insight, and our extensive teaching experience find solutions for our students. ConnectPrep is about connecting - connecting students, parents, educators, and the community. We have bold ideas and an eye towards the future.

The Opportunity: We are offering a Data Analyst Internship for qualified candidates. This is a cross-disciplinary role, both instructional and technical. You'll engage with our team of educators to validate and iterate the models that drive our analytics. Our Data Analysts develop an understanding of our algorithms, the technologies and platforms that support our products, and our proprietary educational content. This knowledge will then be synthesized into actionable tests to build modules that will assist in identifying the academic needs of each individual student. You will engage with team members that think outside of the box, with the goal of improving education through the creation of a data infrastructure and insightful analytics.

Requirements

What we are looking for:


Core skills
You enjoy creative problem solving and working with messy, real-world data.
You have strong math/quantitative skills and excellent attention to detail.
You are curious about how data can improve learning and student outcomes.
Academic & technical background
You are studying (or have studied) Applied Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field.
You have some coding experience and a strong interest in technology and AI.
You have experience with at least one of the following:
SQL, Python data tools (Pandas, NumPy, or similar)
Building simple data pipelines or analysis scripts in Python
Excel & analysis skills
You are comfortable doing serious work in Excel, including several of the following:
Advanced formulas and functions
Tables and formatting
Conditional formatting
Advanced charting and visualizations
Pivot tables and pivot reporting
Using Excel productively for analysis, not just basic spreadsheets
Data tables, simulations, and Solver
(Bonus) VBA and macros
AI, data, and analytics work you will support
Work with AI tools and workflows to support data-driven decision-making
Perform data mining and cleaning from multiple sources (web, internal trackers, marketing platforms)
Support projects involving AI and chatbot development for ConnectPrep's programs and website
Conduct SEO and keyword research to identify growth opportunities and track performance
Analyze website analytics (traffic, engagement, conversions) and prepare simple dashboards or summary reports
Assist with data analysis for marketing campaigns, content performance, and business operations
Help test, document, and iterate on new data and AI workflows as they are developed
Nice to have
Prior internship or project experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or marketing analytics
Familiarity with web analytics and marketing tools (for example, Google Analytics, GA4, Search Console, Looker Studio, or similar platforms)
Experience visualizing data in tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Data Studio
Experience working with SEO tools or keyword platforms
Experience collaborating in remote or hybrid teams (Slack, Zoom, project management boards)
Benefits

Why join ConnectPrep?


Get in on the ground floor of an exciting new company, with the potential to help shape our direction
Be involved with a team that has bold ideas, with the goal of reinventing education across the globe
Gain valuable experience, meet technology innovators, and learn about the business side of our company


Sound Interesting?

Please submit your resume and letter of interest at careers@connectprep.com.

All applicants must be a U.S. Citizen or National of the U.S., an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence, or an alien authorized to work in the U.S. for this employer. ConnectPrep is an equal opportunity employer EEO/M/F/D/V.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst (Contract)

Location: Boston, MA

Job Type: Full-Time | Contract




About SGF Global




SGF Global is an integrated talent solutions provider offering staffing, recruitment, Employer of Record (EOR), nearshore, and consulting services across more than 35 countries.

We connect talented professionals with leading organizations across multiple industries while fostering a collaborative culture that values innovation, professional development, and career growth.




Position Overview




SGF Global is seeking an experienced Data Analyst to support a leading organization in the healthcare and aesthetics industry. This position is ideal for a data-driven professional who enjoys transforming complex business requirements into actionable insights through analytics, reporting, and data architecture.




The ideal candidate will have strong technical expertise in SQL, business intelligence tools, cloud data platforms, and data governance while working closely with both business and IT stakeholders.




Key Responsibilities




Partner with business and IT teams to gather, analyze, and document business requirements.
Translate business needs into technical requirements while collaborating with vendors and implementation teams.
Evaluate current business processes and data infrastructure to improve efficiency and data quality.
Lead software enhancement initiatives and coordinate User Acceptance Testing (UAT).
Design and develop automated dashboards, reports, and visualizations using SQL, Power BI, Tableau, or similar BI tools.
Deliver data-driven insights and recommendations to support operational improvements and product adoption.
Support data modeling, reporting, and continuous process improvement initiatives.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and governance across reporting solutions.




Required Qualifications




5–7 years of experience in Data Analysis, Business Intelligence, or Data Analytics.
Advanced SQL skills with experience querying and managing large datasets.
Strong experience with Tableau, Power BI, or similar data visualization platforms.
Experience working with cloud-based data platforms such as Databricks and Snowflake.
Knowledge of Lakehouse Architecture and modern data warehousing concepts.
Understanding of Master Data Management (MDM) processes.
Experience working in Agile environments.
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Ability to collaborate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders.




Preferred Qualifications




Experience within the pharmaceutical, healthcare, biotechnology, or medical device industries.
Additional experience with enterprise reporting tools or cloud analytics platforms.
Knowledge of data governance best practices and business process optimization.




Why Join SGF Global?




Competitive hourly compensation.
Opportunity to work on impactful data and analytics initiatives.
Collaborative and innovative work environment.
Exposure to modern cloud technologies and business intelligence platforms.
Work alongside experienced professionals supporting enterprise-level projects.
Expand your technical expertise while advancing your career.




If you're passionate about data analytics, business intelligence, and driving meaningful business decisions through data, we'd love to hear from you.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Crown Linen Service, Inc is looking to add an ON-SITE Data Analyst to the team in Brockton, MA!

Join Crown Linen Service Inc as a Full-Time Data Analyst and immerse yourself in an environment where innovation meets excellence. Located in Brockton, MA, this onsite role offers the opportunity to tackle real-world problems and drive strategic initiatives. With a competitive pay range of $80,000 - $90,000, you will be rewarded for your analytical skills and contributions to our forward-thinking team. Experience the thrill of collaborating with passionate professionals dedicated to high performance and integrity in everything they do.

THIS IS NOT A REMOTE POSITION AND REQUIRES DAILY ON-SITE REPORTING TO OUR BROCKTON, MA LOCATION.

You will be provided with great benefits such as Medical, Dental, Vision, 401(k), Life Insurance, Health Savings Account, Flexible Spending Account, Competitive Salary, and Paid Time Off. Don’t miss the chance to shape the future of our services while advancing your career in a supportive and dynamic workplace.

Make a difference as a Data Analyst

We are seeking a Data Analyst to join our data team. We are a small team at a well-established company, giving us the opportunity to make a real and immediate impact. This role focuses on developing reports and automated dashboards to support multiple departments.

What We Offer


A nimble work environment that provides the freedom to explore our data landscape and discover new strategies
An executive team that values the insights that data can provide
A direct impact on the business via reporting, as well as data-driven strategies
A company culture that supports its workforce and is focused on operating an ethical and sustainable business.


What You Bring


Three to five (3-5) years of working experience with progressively more responsibility and advanced work product.
A curious mind - you will often be tasked with vague targets, and it is up to you how to get there.
A collaborative spirit - we touch every aspect of the business and need to be able to work seamlessly across all business functions.
A heightened business acumen - we are not just data folks. We regularly interact with all levels of the organization and must be able to relate our findings to executable business strategies.
A technically savvy approach.
Be an Excel wizard - much like an instrument, you should be able to play Excel like a fiddle
A comfortable working knowledge of how to query data sets across multiple sources with significant exposure to at least one SQL language, including Joins, CTEs, Aggregations and/or Windows Functions (our bread and butter)
The ability to transform vague business requests into concrete data driven solutions
Work experience with at least one BI tool such as PowerBI, Tableau, etc.
The ability to design, implement and maintain an ever-changing ETL landscape

As a new Data Analyst at Crown Linen Service Inc, you can expect a dynamic work environment where each day brings new challenges. Your schedule will be Monday to Friday, with standard business hours dedicated to analyzing data, identifying trends, and providing actionable insights to enhance our services. You will collaborate closely with various departments, facilitating cross-functional meetings to ensure alignment on projects and initiatives. Expect to create and maintain detailed reports, supporting strategic decision-making processes. Your role will involve problem-solving and innovation, requiring you to think critically and propose solutions that drive efficiency.

Additionally, you will participate in ongoing training and development opportunities, allowing you to continuously enhance your skills and knowledge in a high-performance culture.

Make your move

If you're ready to take your Data analyst skills to the next level and join a team that values hard work and your business analyst expertise, apply today!

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment.

Salary: $80000.00 - $90000.00 per year

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
About the Role

We are seeking a highly analytical and detail-oriented Business Analyst/Data Analyst to support a fast-paced Robotics Supply Chain organization. This role offers an exciting opportunity to work within a cutting-edge technology environment while gaining hands-on experience in procurement, supply chain operations, supplier management, and data analytics.

The ideal candidate will possess strong analytical capabilities, advanced Excel skills, and experience working with supply chain, procurement, or business operations data. This position will support ongoing sustaining programs, engineering change management activities, and daily supply-demand analysis for critical components.

Key Responsibilities

Supply Chain & Procurement Support

Support daily supply chain operations and component demand planning activities
Assist with Agile and Engineering Change Order (ECO) management processes
Create and manage purchase requisitions within procurement systems
Track purchase orders and monitor supplier delivery performance
Maintain supplier databases, procurement records, and documentation
Coordinate supplier communications regarding quotes, deliveries, and documentation

Data Analysis & Reporting

Analyze supply and demand requirements for components
Create reports, dashboards, pivot tables, and spend analyses using Excel
Monitor supplier performance metrics, including on-time delivery
Maintain pricing databases and cost comparison models
Generate regular business reports and project status updates

Supplier & Cross-Functional Coordination

Coordinate sample requests and supplier interactions with engineering teams
Assist with supplier onboarding and documentation management
Participate in supplier meetings and cross-functional project discussions
Support RFQ (Request for Quote) preparation and supplier evaluations

Research & Continuous Improvement

Conduct supplier and market research
Compile competitive intelligence and pricing benchmarks
Support cost reduction and supplier consolidation initiatives
Help document and improve procurement processes and procedures

Quality & Compliance Support

Track supplier quality metrics and performance indicators
Maintain supplier certification and compliance documentation
Assist with audit preparation and corrective action tracking




Required Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Supply Chain, Operations, Logistics, Transportation, Business Analytics, or a related field
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills, including pivot tables, formulas, and data analysis
Experience working with business, operational, supply chain, or procurement data
Strong organizational skills with exceptional attention to detail
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment




Preferred Qualifications

Experience with ERP systems (Oracle preferred)
Experience in supply chain, procurement, operations, or business analysis
Knowledge of configuration management concepts
Experience with SQL and data querying tools
Familiarity with supplier management or commodity management processes
Exposure to manufacturing, engineering, or robotics environments

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Marketing Analytics
Description:
RSAC is the premier series of global events and year‑round learning for the cybersecurity community. RSAC is where the security industry converges to discuss current and future concerns and gain access to experts, unbiased content, and ideas that help individuals and companies advance their cybersecurity posture and build stronger, smarter teams. 

Both in‑person and online, RSAC brings the cybersecurity industry together and empowers the collective “we” to stand against cyberthreats around the world. RSAC is the ultimate marketplace for the latest technologies and hands‑on educational opportunities, helping industry professionals discover how to make their organisations more secure while showcasing the most enterprising, influential, and thought‑provoking thinkers and leaders in cybersecurity today. 

RSAC is seeking a highly analytical, business-minded Senior Data Analyst, Marketing Analytics to serve as the dedicated analytics partner supporting both Conference and Membership Marketing initiatives. 

This role will be responsible for transforming marketing data into actionable insights that drive acquisition, engagement, retention, and revenue growth. The ideal candidate combines strong technical expertise with exceptional business acumen and stakeholder management skills. They are comfortable moving between strategic discussions with marketing leadership and hands-on analytical execution. 

The Senior Data Analyst will partner closely with Marketing, Conference, Membership, and Data & Analytics teams to build reporting frameworks, measure campaign effectiveness, develop attribution models, identify growth opportunities, and provide executive-level insights that influence business decisions. 

This role reports into the Data & Analytics organization while serving as the primary analytical partner to Marketing. 

What You’ll Do 

Marketing Performance & Campaign Analytics 

Serve as the primary analytics partner for Marketing, supporting both Conference and Membership business lines. 

Develop and maintain reporting frameworks for marketing campaign performance across channels. 

Analyze marketing effectiveness and provide actionable recommendations to improve acquisition, engagement, and retention outcomes. 

Measure campaign ROI and support data-driven budget allocation decisions. 

Establish standardized marketing KPI definitions and reporting methodologies. 

Attribution & Funnel Analytics 

Develop and maintain marketing attribution frameworks across Conference and Membership initiatives. 

Analyze acquisition channels, conversion funnels, and customer journeys to identify opportunities for optimization. 

Partner with Marketing stakeholders to improve lead generation, conversion, and audience growth strategies. 

Evaluate performance across paid, owned, and earned marketing channels. 

Conference Marketing Analytics 

Support conference registration reporting and performance measurement. 

Analyze registration funnel performance and identify conversion opportunities. 

Measure effectiveness of conference marketing campaigns and promotional activities. 

Support sponsorship analytics and attendee engagement reporting. 

Provide insights that help drive conference growth and improve attendee experience. 

Membership Marketing Analytics 

Analyze subscriber acquisition, activation, retention, and lifetime value metrics. 

Measure performance of email campaigns, content marketing initiatives, and member engagement efforts. 

Identify audience behaviors, engagement patterns, and opportunities to improve member retention and growth. 

Partner with Marketing to develop actionable audience segmentation strategies. 

Executive Reporting & Insights 

Design and maintain executive dashboards that provide visibility into marketing performance and business outcomes. 

Translate complex analytical findings into clear recommendations for senior leadership. 

Deliver recurring performance reviews and strategic insights to Marketing leadership and executive stakeholders. 

Serve as a trusted advisor and strategic thought partner to Marketing leadership. 

Data Analysis & Visualization 

Write complex SQL queries to analyze large datasets and answer business questions. 

Develop and maintain dashboards and self-service reporting solutions using Tableau. 

Partner with Data Engineering and Analytics Engineering teams to improve data quality, accessibility, and reporting capabilities. 

Support development of scalable reporting and measurement frameworks. 

Experimentation & Strategic Analytics 

Support marketing testing and experimentation initiatives through measurement design and analysis. 

Develop audience segmentation methodologies to improve targeting and personalization. 

Conduct ad hoc analyses to support strategic business decisions. 

Identify trends, opportunities, and risks through proactive analysis and stakeholder engagement. 

Application Requirements 

Core Requirements 

Bachelor's degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Marketing Analytics, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field. 

5–7 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, marketing analytics, or related analytical roles. 

Advanced SQL skills with the ability to independently analyze large and complex datasets. 

Experience building dashboards and visualizations using Tableau or similar BI tools. 

Experience working with cloud data platforms such as Snowflake. 

Strong understanding of marketing analytics, campaign measurement, attribution, and performance reporting. 

Experience with audience segmentation, customer lifecycle analytics, retention analysis, cohort analysis and lifetime value modeling. 

Experience communicating analytical findings to business stakeholders and senior leaders. 

Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to translate business questions into analytical solutions. 

Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 

Ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders in a fast-paced environment. 

Bonus Points  

Experience supporting marketing, growth, membership, subscription, media, SaaS, or event-based businesses. 

Familiarity with Google Analytics (GA4) and digital marketing measurement concepts. 

Familiarity with dbt and modern data stack technologies. 

Exposure to Python or other analytical programming languages. 

Experience supporting conference, event, or membership-based organizations. 

Experience designing and measuring A/B tests and marketing experiments. 

Success in This Role 

Within the first year, a successful candidate will: 

Establish themselves as the trusted analytics partner for Conference and Membership Marketing teams. 

Independently own marketing analytics initiatives with minimal supervision. 

Deliver executive-ready reporting and dashboards that drive business decisions. 

Implement scalable marketing performance and attribution reporting frameworks. 

Improve reporting speed, consistency, and analytical rigor across marketing functions. 

Provide strategic insights that influence acquisition, engagement, retention, and revenue growth initiatives. 

Earn the confidence of stakeholders as a trusted advisor and thought partner. 

Location & Eligibility 

Please be aware that although this is a remote position, to be considered for the vacancy you must have residency in one of the following states: 

California, Colorado, Florida, Kansas, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Washington 

Benefits 

RSAC believes in investing in our people. We offer: 

Salary range: $110 – $125 

Employer‑subsidised medical, dental, and vision insurance 

401K retirement employer match 

Home office equipment stipend and monthly technology stipend 

Thirteen paid holidays per calendar year 

Flexible personal time off 

Annual employee bonus dependent on company and personal performance 

Annual company‑wide offsite 

Our Culture 

We believe that our differences make us stronger, and we are committed to fostering a culture of respect, empathy, and understanding. 

We are a fully remote team operating across the United States, giving our employees the flexibility to work from wherever they choose. 

Our team is passionate and results‑oriented, striving to achieve excellence in everything we do. 

We strongly believe in creating an inclusive environment that values diversity and encourages our team members to share their unique perspectives. 

We recognise that by collaborating and working together, we can achieve our goals faster and more effectively. 

Why RSAC? 

The RSAC team takes great pride in helping shape the future of cybersecurity and being part of an expansive global community. We’re always looking for imaginative and visionary individuals who share our passion for providing cutting‑edge programs that equip cybersecurity practitioners worldwide with the intel and knowledge they need to thrive and safeguard organisations against cyberthreats. 

Our Values 

Adaptability: In our ever‑changing world, we innovate through determination, creativity, and resourcefulness. 

Community: We bring people together and build trust by embracing the unique thoughts and perspectives of others with kindness and respect. 

Excellence: Because we are where the world talks security, we have the highest expectations of ourselves and our partners. 

Equal Opportunity Statement 

RSAC is an equal opportunity employer committed to inclusion and diversity. We take affirmative action to ensure all qualified applicants receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics.

Title: SIOP Data Analyst - Direct Hire
Description:
Senior Data Analyst

Department: Office of the COO

Reports To: SIOP Manager

Location: Full‑time Onsite — MetroWest Boston Area

Schedule: 5 days onsite (future potential for 4 onsite / 1 remote)

Target Salary Range: $80,000 – $120,000




About the Company (Disguised)

Our client is a leading provider of advanced manufacturing and automation solutions, operating multiple technology centers across North America. The organization specializes in custom machining systems, automation integration, and turnkey production solutions, supporting a wide range of industrial and high‑precision manufacturing customers.

Position Summary

The Senior Data Analyst is a key analytical partner within the Office of the COO, responsible for transforming complex operational, sales, and service data into strategic insights that drive business performance. This role leads the development of enterprise dashboards, reporting frameworks, and data models that support SIOP, sales operations, and aftermarket service functions.

The analyst collaborates closely with cross‑functional leaders to deliver data‑driven recommendations that enhance revenue growth, operational efficiency, and continuous improvement initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the collection, integration, and analysis of data from CRM, ERP, and enterprise systems to support sales performance, aftermarket services, and SIOP planning cycles.
Design and maintain advanced dashboards and KPI reports across equipment sales, service revenue, parts consumption, and forecasting accuracy.
Serve as the primary analytics partner for SIOP, providing demand/supply analysis, forecast variance reporting, and scenario modeling for executive decision‑making.
Develop standardized aftermarket analytics, including service contract utilization, customer lifecycle metrics, and parts‑usage trends.
Perform complex statistical analyses to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for operational and revenue optimization.
Ensure data integrity and governance through rigorous validation, documentation, and quality‑assurance practices.
Collaborate with Sales, Operations, Finance, and Aftermarket teams to translate business needs into scalable, automated reporting solutions.
Lead ad‑hoc analytical projects and present insights and recommendations to senior leadership.

Required Qualifications

Education: Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Computer Science, Business Administration, or related field (or equivalent experience).
Experience: 2–7 years in data analysis, business intelligence, or analytics—preferably in manufacturing, distribution, or industrial sales environments.

Technical Skills

Advanced proficiency with Excel, Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualization tools.
Strong SQL skills for complex data extraction and transformation.
Experience with Python, R, or similar analytical programming languages.
Familiarity with ERP/CRM platforms such as Infor CSI, Salesforce, ServiceNow, SAP, or similar systems.
Understanding of SIOP/S&OP planning tools and processes.

Soft Skills

Ability to synthesize complex data into clear, actionable insights for technical and non‑technical audiences.
Strong critical‑thinking and problem‑solving abilities.
Excellent organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment.
Proven cross‑functional collaboration and stakeholder‑management capabilities.

Supervisory Responsibilities

No direct reports; mentorship of junior analysts may be expected.

Travel Requirements

Minimal; occasional travel to regional facilities.

Physical Requirements

Extended periods of computer‑based work.
Ability to lift up to 20 lbs as needed.
