Title: Senior Analyst, Data and Analysis
Description:
Company Description

Digitas is the Networked Experience Agency, built on the vision that we create magnetic experiences that earn the right for brands to exist in human networks. Today, and tomorrow. We deliver Networked Experiences by leveraging comprehensive data, technology, creative, media and strategy capabilities. Digitas delivers ambitious outcomes via unique solutions that include Creative Experiences, Integrated Media, Addressable Relationships, Social Marketing and Total Commerce. Celebrated by AdAge as Data and Insights Agency of the Year, U.S Campaign’s Brand Experience Agency of the Year, Media Network of the Year and celebrated by Forrester and Gartner, Digitas serves the world’s leading brands through a global network comprised of more than 5,500 employees across over 65 offices in 43 countries.

The Digitas culture is made up of fearless, inventive and generous Unicorns of all kinds.

Overview

The Data & Analysis team uses data-driven insights to fuel strategic growth for clients. We believe that data should never exist in a vacuum; instead, it should be put to work to bring the best ideas and stories to our clients.

To help with this, we’re looking for an outstanding Senior Analyst – someone who can analyze massive data sets, make insightful cross-channel recommendations, and communicate strategic ideas to clients. Sound like you? Read on.

As a Senior Analyst, you’ll build the strategic and tactical foundation for data-driven marketing efforts. You’ll work to achieve client goals through brand and acquisition campaigns, site optimization, and/or online testing strategy.

Responsibilities

Day-to-day, your role includes:


Instituting frameworks across project and campaign lifecycles, including measurement plans, primary and secondary research, and performance reporting
Diving into large data sets, uncovering insights, and providing impactful recommendations to clients
Connecting the dots across channels through data (including display media, site, search, email, and social) to provide a holistic view of marketing efforts
Communicating with clients regularly on scheduled deliverables and ad-hoc requests
Collaborating with internal teams and external partners to drive success for client’s business goals


Qualifications


3-5 years of experience in analytics or strategy
A four-year college degree
Passion for digital marketing and emerging trends in the digital landscape
Experience communicating with internal and external stakeholders at a variety of levels
Fluency with Excel (including pivot tables and vlookups) and/or other data analysis tools such as SQL or Tableau
Comfortable in a fast-paced and deadline-driven environment
Familiarity with prompt-based interaction and commonly used generative AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Google Gemini, DALL·E, Midjourney) is a plus, especially for tasks like ideation, research, or content generation.


Got the sparkle? Apply if you believe your unique skills are a fit.

Additional Information

Our Publicis Groupe motto “Viva La Différence” means we’re better together, and we believe that our differences make us stronger. It means we honor and celebrate all identities, across all facets of intersectionality, and it underpins all that we do as an organization. We are focused on fostering belonging and creating equitable & inclusive experiences for all talent.

Publicis Groupe provides robust and inclusive benefit programs and policies to support the evolving and diverse needs of our talent and enable every person to grow and thrive. Celebrated by AdAge as Data and Insights Agency of the Year, U.S Campaign’s Brand Experience Agency of the Year, Media Network of the Year and celebrated by Forrester and Gartner, Digitas serves the world’s leading brands through a global network comprised of more than 5,500 employees across over 65 offices in 43 countries.

The Digitas culture is made up of fearless, inventive and generous Unicorns of all kinds.

Overview

The Data & Analysis team uses data-driven insights to fuel strategic growth for clients. We believe that data should never exist in a vacuum; instead, it should be put to work to bring the best ideas and stories to our clients.

To help with this, we’re looking for an outstanding Senior Analyst – someone who can analyze massive data sets, make insightful cross-channel recommendations, and communicate strategic ideas to clients. Sound like you? Read on.

As a Senior Analyst, you’ll build the strategic and tactical foundation for data-driven marketing efforts. You’ll work to achieve client goals through brand and acquisition campaigns, site optimization, and/or online testing strategy.

Responsibilities

Day-to-day, your role includes:

  • Instituting frameworks across project and campaign lifecycles, including measurement plans, primary and secondary research, and performance reporting
  • Diving into large data sets, uncovering insights, and providing impactful recommendations to clients
  • Connecting the dots across channels through data (including display media, site, search, email, and social) to provide a holistic view of marketing efforts
  • Communicating with clients regularly on scheduled deliverables and ad-hoc requests
  • Collaborating with internal teams and external partners to drive success for client’s business goals

Qualifications

  • Familiarity with prompt-based interaction and commonly used generative AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Google Gemini, DALL·E, Midjourney) is a plus, especially for tasks like ideation, research, or content generation.

Title: MDM Analyst
Description:
Job Title: MDM Analyst – Service Integration (SI) & Master Data Management (MDM)

Location: Boston, MA (Hybrid/Onsite as per client requirement)

Duration: 12+ Months




We are seeking an experienced Business Data Analyst (BA) with strong Service Integration (SI) and Master Data Management (MDM) experience to support a new Service Integration initiative for our client, Vertex. The ideal candidate will work closely with business and technical stakeholders to gather, analyze, and document requirements while ensuring seamless integration across services, business processes, and enterprise master data.




Key Responsibilities

Collaborate with business stakeholders to gather and analyze business requirements for Service Integration and MDM initiatives.
Translate business needs into functional requirements, user stories, and process documentation.
Work with cross-functional teams to support Service Integration planning, execution, and MDM implementation.
Analyze and document master data requirements, data governance processes, and data quality standards.
Conduct gap analysis, process mapping, and workflow optimization.
Facilitate workshops, meetings, and requirement review sessions with business and technical stakeholders.
Support testing activities, including UAT planning, execution, and defect tracking.
Ensure project deliverables align with business objectives, integration standards, and MDM best practices.




Required Skills & Experience

Proven experience as a Business Analyst.
Hands-on experience with Service Integration (SI) projects is mandatory.
Hands-on experience with Master Data Management (MDM) initiatives is mandatory.
Strong expertise in requirements gathering, business process analysis, and documentation.
Experience working with enterprise master data domains (e.g., Customer, Product, Vendor, or Supplier data).
Understanding of data governance, data quality, and master data lifecycle management.
Experience working with cross-functional business and technical teams.
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.
Familiarity with Agile/Scrum methodologies is preferred.




Preferred Qualifications

Experience working on enterprise integration, digital transformation, or data management initiatives.
Exposure to MDM platforms such as Informatica MDM, Reltio, SAP MDG, IBM InfoSphere MDM, or similar solutions is a plus.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About The Role

CodeVertex Careers is hiring a Data Analyst to support reporting, data analysis, business insights, and data quality workflows. This role helps turn structured information into clear, useful reporting for operational decision-making.

Responsibilities


Collect, clean, organize, and validate data for reporting and operational review.
Build or maintain dashboards, spreadsheets, reports, and documentation.
Identify trends, inconsistencies, gaps, and opportunities for process improvement.
Communicate findings clearly through summaries, charts, and structured updates.
Maintain data quality, repeatable reporting workflows, and accurate records.


Required Qualifications


Strong attention to detail and comfort working with spreadsheets, reports, and data.
Ability to organize information clearly and explain findings in practical terms.
Experience with Excel, Google Sheets, SQL, dashboards, or reporting tools preferred.
Reliable communication and documentation skills.
Ability to work independently while maintaining accuracy and consistency.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience supporting remote teams or documentation-heavy workflows.
Comfort using common workplace tools such as spreadsheets, email, calendars, CRM, ATS, ticketing, or reporting systems depending on role.
Ability to maintain accuracy across recurring tasks and internal systems.


Work Environment


Remote role for candidates located in the United States.
Full-time or part-time classification is shown in the job details.
Individual contributor or coordination-based responsibilities depending on role.
Role-specific access, systems, and training are provided according to business requirements.


Equal Opportunity Statement

CodeVertex Careers is an equal opportunity employer. We consider qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, veteran status, age, or any other protected status under applicable law.

Title: Data Entry Clerk/ Virtual Assistant
Description:
About The Company

Evolution Sports Group is a dynamic and innovative organization dedicated to excellence in the sports industry. With a focus on providing exceptional services and solutions, the company has established a strong reputation for its commitment to quality, professionalism, and growth. Our team is composed of passionate professionals who strive to deliver outstanding results and foster a collaborative work environment. As we continue to expand our operations, we are seeking talented individuals to join our team and contribute to our ongoing success.

About The Role

We are looking for a highly organized and detail-oriented Data Entry Clerk/Virtual Assistant to become a vital part of our remote team. This full-time position offers the flexibility to work from your own home while supporting our administrative and data management needs. The ideal candidate will possess excellent data entry skills, strong attention to detail, and the ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment. Your primary responsibilities will include maintaining accurate data records, managing electronic and physical files, assisting with scheduling, and providing general administrative support to ensure smooth operations across the organization. This role provides an excellent opportunity for professional growth and to be part of a forward-thinking company that values its employees.

Qualifications


High School diploma or equivalent
1-2 years of experience in data entry or administrative support
Proficient in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and Google Suite (Docs, Sheets, Slides)
Strong attention to detail and high level of accuracy
Excellent communication and organizational skills
Ability to work independently and meet deadlines
Previous experience in the sports industry is a plus but not mandatory
Reliable internet connection and home office setup


Responsibilities


Enter and maintain accurate data in our database systems
Organize and manage electronic and physical files efficiently
Respond promptly to emails and phone inquiries
Assist with scheduling, calendar management, and appointment setting
Conduct research and gather relevant information as needed
Prepare reports, presentations, and other documentation
Perform various administrative tasks as assigned by management
Support team members with ad hoc projects and tasks


Benefits


Competitive hourly pay rate ranging from $35 to $55, based on experience
Work from home flexibility and a remote work environment
Paid training at $30 per hour, with a $700 incentive upon successful completion
Paid time off and holidays
Health, dental, and vision insurance coverage
Retirement savings plan to support your financial future
Opportunities for professional development and career advancement
Company-provided workstation and equipment to optimize your home office setup
Flexible full-time or part-time work schedules to suit your lifestyle


Equal Opportunity

Evolution Sports Group is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, gender, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected status. We encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply and join our team.

Title: Research Analyst
Description:
Research Analyst




Location: United States




Employment Type: Full-Time




Job Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Research Analyst to conduct research, analyze data, and generate insights that support business strategy and decision-making. The successful candidate will gather information from multiple sources, evaluate market trends, prepare reports, and present findings to stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities
Conduct qualitative and quantitative research using internal and external data sources.
Collect, analyze, and interpret data to identify trends and business opportunities.
Prepare research reports, presentations, and executive summaries.
Perform market, industry, and competitor analysis.
Develop and maintain databases, dashboards, and research documentation.
Monitor market trends and provide actionable recommendations.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support strategic initiatives.
Verify the accuracy and reliability of research findings.
Present insights and recommendations to management and stakeholders.
Support ad hoc research projects as assigned.
Required Qualifications
Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Economics, Finance, Statistics, Market Research, Data Analytics, Social Sciences, or a related field.
1–3 years of experience in research, data analysis, business analysis, or a related analytical role.
Strong analytical and critical-thinking skills.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.
Experience with data analysis and reporting tools.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Strong attention to detail and organizational abilities.
Ability to manage multiple research projects and deadlines.
Preferred Qualifications
Experience with SQL, Power BI, Tableau, SPSS, or similar analytical tools.
Knowledge of statistical analysis and research methodologies.
Experience conducting market research or competitive analysis.
Familiarity with survey tools and data visualization platforms.
Experience working with large datasets and business intelligence tools.

• $25–$30 per hour

• Fully remote work

2. Aout the Opportunity:

The Remote Data Entry Specialist plays a crucial role in maintaining the accuracy and organization of information within company databases and systems. This position contributes to the overall data quality and operational efficiency by ensuring data is entered and verified correctly. The role offers remote work flexibility, paid training, and opportunities for professional development.


3. Responsibilities:

• Enter and update data accurately into databases and spreadsheets

• Review records to identify errors, inconsistencies, or missing information

• Organize and maintain digital files and documentation

• Verify data accuracy through cross-checking source materials

• Communicate with internal teams to resolve data discrepancies

• Adhere to confidentiality and data protection procedures

• Achieve daily and weekly productivity targets


4. Requirements:

• High school diploma or equivalent

• Strong attention to detail and organizational skills

• Basic computer skills and typing proficiency

• Ability to work independently in a remote environment

• Effective communication and time-management skills

• Reliable internet connection and access to a computer or laptop

• Previous data entry, administrative, or customer support experience preferred but not required

• Familiarity with Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, and online databases is a plus


5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• $25–$30 per hour

• Weekly pay available depending on employer policies


6. Benefits & Perks:

• Fully remote work

• Flexible scheduling options

• Paid training

• Career advancement opportunities

• Supportive and collaborative work environment

• Work-life balance with remote flexibility

Title: Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Apply online at https://www.governmentjobs.com/careers/williams

Intelligence Analyst

The Intelligence Analyst performs technical, analytical, and administrative work in support of the Northwest Narcotics Task Force. This position is responsible for researching, compiling, and analyzing criminal intelligence data from various sources to identify trends and assist in criminal investigations.

Examples Of Duties

The intent of this job description is to provide a representative summary of the primary duties and responsibilities performed by incumbents in this position. The duties and responsibilities described are not a comprehensive list and may change with or without notice. Incumbents must be able to perform the essential function of the position with or without reasonable accommodation.


Utilizes specialized computer applications to gather and analyze criminal intelligence data
Tracks department statistics and completes required reporting, such as Uniform Crime Reporting (UCR) and grants, according to guidelines
Develops reports, charts, and other presentations based on the analysis of intelligence data
Maintains a digital library of departmental evidentiary files, photographs, and audio records; provides copies to courts, attorneys, and the public following established policies and laws
Administers the RISS Money Counter Network system for the Northwest Narcotics Task Force
Assists law enforcement in obtaining pen registers, processing received information, and conducting analysis
Utilizes Cellebrite software for data extraction and analysis to further investigations involving multiple agencies
Validates intelligence data using open-source or confidential data sources
Assists in maintaining custody, inventory control, and safeguards for all items of evidence
Follows established laws, standards, and guidelines, including, but not limited to, chain of custody, evidence access requests, shipping and transporting procedures, and retention periods
Maintains accurate documentation of all cases and intelligence
Provides testimony in court regarding the evidence process and information provided
Assists with record checks and general clerical duties as needed
Maintains confidentiality of non-public records and makes available all data and information deemed a public record by the North Dakota Century Code.


Typical Qualifications


Associate degree in criminal justice, public administration, social science, computer science, business administration, or related field OR an equivalent combination of education, training, and experience
Must possess a valid North Dakota Driver’s License or equivalent
Must obtain and maintain security certification from the North Dakota Bureau of Criminal Investigations
Must pass a thorough background check and pre-employment drug and alcohol screening


Supplemental Information

Work is intermittently performed in a windowless storage facility and off-site internal and external environments with exposure to inclement weather, varying temperatures, and noxious fumes; use of personal protective equipment (PPE) will be required when handling items known or suspected to be harboring contamination from chemicals, bodily fluids, bio-hazardous materials, drugs, flammables, and otherwise unidentified contaminants. Transportation of evidence to regional centers may sporadically require travel to destinations within 250 miles of Williston.

Compensation: Starting pay ranges from $25.98 to $28.71 per hour, based on qualifications and experience.

Benefits

Williams County offers a generous benefit package including paid sick and vacation leave, fully paid health, dental, and vision insurance plan for single, single plus dependent, or family contracts, term life insurance, long-term disability insurance, fully funded retirement benefits through the North Dakota Public Employee Retirement System, and more!

To learn more about Williams County, please visit our website: www.williamsnd.com/departments/county-information/

To learn more about living and working in Williams County, visit whereinwilliamscounty.com.
